The decipherment of hieroglyphic writing was finally accomplished in the 1820s by Jean-François Champollion, with the help of the Rosetta Stone.[8] The number of words contained in al Ancient Egyptian (i.e. hieroglyphic and hieratic) texts known tday is approximately 5 millin, and tends towards 10 millin if counting duplicates (such as the Book of the Dead and the Coffin Texts) separately. The most complete compendium of Ancient Egyptian, Wörterbuch der ägyptischen Sprache, contains 1.5–1.7 millin words.[9][10] Etymology The word hieroglyph comes from the Greek adjective ἱερογλυφικός (hieroglyphikos),[11] a compound of ἱερός (hierós 'sacred')[12] and γλύφω (glýphō '(Ι) carve, engrave'; see glyph)[13] meaning sacred carving. The glyphs themselves, since the Ptolemaic period, were called τὰ ἱερογλυφικὰ [γράμματα] (tà hieroglyphikà [grámmata]) "the sacred engraved letters", the Greek counterpart to the Egyptian expression of mdw.w-nṯr "god's words".[14] Greek ἱερόγλυφος meant "a carver of hieroglyphs".[15] In English, hieroglyph as a noun is recorded from 1590, originally short for nominalized hieroglyphic (1580s, with a plural hieroglyphics), from adjectival use (hieroglyphic character).[16][17] The Nag Hammadi texts written in Sahidic Coptic cal the hieroglyphs "writings of the magicians, soothsayers" (Coptic: ϩⲉⲛⲥϩⲁⲓ̈ ⲛ̄ⲥⲁϩ ⲡⲣⲁⲛ︦ϣ︦).[18] | | | | | | Both President Trump and Elon Musk are warning about something that could send the market down 50%, real estate down 40% and savings accounts down 30%. | | | Sponsored Content | • | Salem Media Group ---|--- • | Behind the Markets • | Safe Money Report | | | | | brоught tо уоu bу **Іnception Media, LLC. tо stоp rеcеiving mаrkеting cоmmunicаtiоn frоm us. Fееl frее tо еmаil ussupport@firstinvestmentupdates.com 600 N Broad St Ste 5 PMB 1 Middletown, DE 19709 Іnception Media, LLC. Аll rights rеsеrvеd ** | | |

# _It’s Time to Take Back Harris County SWAMP!_
---
| | | | | | | | | | | |

**This election, Harris County faces a simple choice:****keep the waste, the deficits, and the chaos—or choose a leader who knows how to fix it.**
I’ve spent my career building businesses, balancing budgets, and delivering results in the real world.
While the current administration grows government and weakens public safety, I’m ready to streamline departments, support law enforcement, repair failing infrastructure, and return accountability to county government.
Republican voters know what’s at stake. We need a leader who won’t back down, won’t make excuses, and **won’t let Harris County slide any further.**
[_**👉 www.howellforharriscounty.com.**_](https://howellforharriscounty.us4.list-manage.com/track/click?u=ab8c3a643a45a6e2b06d8f050&id=2980ec9d29&e=7c92d84deb)

It’s time to restore order, strengthen our economy, and make this county work for the people again.
My administration will DOGE Harris County and deliver a smaller County government that is highly efficient and reflective of the rigorous economy that Texans deserve.
* **It's time for the FUTURE. **
* **It's time for change.**
* **It's time to take back Harris County!**

See My full plan to _MAKE HARRIS COUNTY GREAT AGAIN_ on the campaign website.
Join our winning team and help lead Harris County to a brighter future with leadership that **WORKS FOR THE PEOPLE!**

name: Heidi Ganahl
subject: The Mountain Minute: 12/16/2025
body:
The Bill of Rights was written to limit power. One civics lesson explains how.
| | | | [](https://link.email.joinrmv.)
---
**PRESENTED BY[ROCKYMOUNTAINVOICE.COM](https://link.email.joinrmv.)**
| | Hi friends,
Yesterday marked **Bill of Rights Day** , a reminder that our freedoms were designed to limit government power—not protect it. _[Read this article to celebrate!](https://link.email.joinrmv.)_ Also be sure to watch the video version of our Sunday News Digest, where we walk through the Tina Peters case in full, explaining what happened, why it matters, and how constitutional protections have been tested along the way: _[Facebook](https://link.email.joinrmv.) | [Instagram](https://link.email.joinrmv.) | [TikTok](https://link.email.joinrmv.) | [X](https://link.email.joinrmv.)_
And if you need a lighter moment, check out our **12 Days of Dog (and Kitty!) Christmas** on social—featuring adoptable pets across Colorado who are hoping to find a home for the holidays. 🎄
Listen to today's Mountain Minute podcast [here](https://link.email.joinrmv.), or watch on [YouTube](https://link.email.joinrmv.) or [Rumble](https://link.email.joinrmv.). Here is a summary of today's top stories:
🏔️ **Here in Colorado...** Outrage followed after a repeat offender—released despite a long history of domestic violence—was accused of killing a mother of three, refocusing attention on [Colorado’s lenient criminal-justice system](https://link.email.joinrmv.). Economists also warned of a [slow recovery as policy-driven setbacks continue](https://link.email.joinrmv.) to drag down major job sectors, while a massive Medicaid fraud case in Minnesota served as a [warning for Colorado](https://link.email.joinrmv.) as lawmakers push expanded social spending. Even so, Coloradans showed generosity and service, with deputies delivering nearly [2,000 pounds of food to an Evergreen pantry](https://link.email.joinrmv.) and volunteer pilots [airlifting holiday supplies to veterans](https://link.email.joinrmv.) statewide.
🇺🇸**On the National Stage...** The country mourned the loss of Brown University student Ella Cook in a campus shooting, renewing concerns about [violence and cultural breakdown on college campuses](https://link.email.joinrmv.). Bill of Rights Day also sparked renewed focus on [constitutional limits on government power](https://link.email.joinrmv.), with a civics lesson warning that modern socialist policies clash with the freedoms protected by the Founders.
🗣️**Commentary Spotlight...** Commentary today warns of an [ideological takeover in schools](https://link.email.joinrmv.), cautions Republicans against an [“extinction-level” failure to govern](https://link.email.joinrmv.), questions whether voters were [misled on Propositions LL and MM](https://link.email.joinrmv.), and argues Colorado’s [climate-driven regulatory shift](https://link.email.joinrmv.) is quietly sidelining working families and rural communities.
This is the Mountain Minute for Tuesday, December 16, 2025.
– Team RMV
---
|
---
**TODAY'S TOP STORIES**
**[The Bill of Rights was written to limit power. One civics lesson explains how.](https://link.email.joinrmv.)** BY JEN SCHUMANN, [ROCKY MOUNTAIN VOICE](https://link.email.joinrmv.)
[](https://link.email.joinrmv.)
Bill of Rights Day is often marked with references to free speech, due process and other familiar rights. Less attention is paid to the reason those protections exist at all: to place clear limits on government power.
That question sits at the center of a handwritten civics lesson now being shared among homeschool students, one that walks through how the Constitution was designed to restrict government authority, including economic decision-making.
Susie Dean, a homeschool civics educator, centered one of her recent lessons on basic but often overlooked questions. What authority does the Constitution actually give government? And what does it leave off the table?
**[READ MORE](https://link.email.joinrmv.)**
|
---
**[The Modern Cult Operating in Plain Sight in Our Schools](https://link.email.joinrmv.)** BY LAUREEN BOLL, [ROCKY MOUNTAIN VOICE](https://link.email.joinrmv.)
* **Public schools are acting like state-sanctioned cults that undermine parental authority:** The commentary argues that modern school systems mirror the tactics of 1970s predatory cults—isolating children from parents, redefining authority, and replacing family guidance with ideological control under the banner of “inclusion” and “belonging.”
* **The Healthy Kids Colorado Survey is portrayed as ideological grooming—not neutral data collection:** Administered without mandatory parental consent, the survey probes children on gender identity, sexuality, mental health, and family dynamics, which the author says plants seeds of doubt, normalizes distress, and shapes beliefs rather than simply measuring them.
* **Opt-in consent is a last line of defense for parents:** The piece defends Douglas County’s move to require explicit parental permission for the survey, warning that progressive efforts to reverse it prioritize grant money and political agendas over families’ constitutional right to direct their children’s upbringing.

**[READ MORE](https://link.email.joinrmv.)**
|

The only way we can continue to spread truth is with your support!
[DONATE TO ROCKY MOUNTAIN VOICE](https://link.email.joinrmv.)
|
---
**[Minnesota’s Medicaid Scandal Shows Why Colorado Must Tighten the Guardrails](https://link.email.joinrmv.)** [COMPLETE COLORADO](https://link.email.joinrmv.)
* **Minnesota’s Medicaid expansion exploded into massive fraud:** COVID-era waivers meant to fund housing and social services ballooned from an estimated $2.6 million per year to more than $300 million, with federal prosecutors saying most of the spending was fraudulent—exposing how quickly “emergency” government programs can spiral out of control.
* **Looser oversight invited criminal abuse of taxpayer dollars:** The article warns that relaxed eligibility rules and weak verification systems created a perfect environment for fraud, as politically popular programs expanded faster than the government’s ability—or willingness—to police them.
* **Colorado risks repeating the same mistakes:** As state leaders push to grow Medicaid and social spending, the Minnesota case is framed as a clear warning that without strict guardrails, transparency, and accountability, well-intended programs can become pipelines for abuse at taxpayers’ expense.

**[READ MORE](https://link.email.joinrmv.)**
|
---
**ONLY ON ROCKY MOUNTAIN VOICE ⤵️ **
[**Colorado’s quiet transformation leaves working communities behind**](https://link.email.joinrmv.)
**BY SCOTT JAMES | COMMENTARY | DECEMBER 15** _I am sounding the alarm on the quiet erosion of Colorado’s values, warning of a top-down agenda that’s silencing everyday citizens. Not the Colorado of glossy tourism ads and climate conferences. The real Colorado._ __
[**An extinction level event looms for the Republican Party**](https://link.email.joinrmv.)
**BY BRIAN JOONDEPH | COMMENTARY | DECEMBER 15** _An extinction event is a rapid, sweeping collapse — something so disruptive that what emerges afterward is unrecognizable from what came before. Volcano eruptions or meteor strikes can trigger such events in the natural world. Washington, D.C. may be approaching a political version of the same phenomenon, and Republicans seem disturbingly unprepared for what is coming._ __
[**Growing Up in Prayer — Why It Matters for the Armor of God**](https://link.email.joinrmv.)
[****](https://link.email.joinrmv.)**BY DRAKE HUNTER | COMMENTARY | DECEMBER 14** _This week, our family celebrated a special milestone: my 9-month-old grandson, Thommy-boy, said his very first word—“Baby.” Moments like that remind us how wonderful life can be even during tough times. Later, I thought: What if, ten years from now, that was still the only word he could say?_ __

body:
[ https://act.abdulformichigan.com/go/30?refcode=&t=1001&akid=1433%2E251025%2EK6904b ]Abdul for US Senate [ https://act.abdulformichigan.com/go/30?refcode=&t=1002&akid=1433%2E251025%2EK6904b ]Abdul for US Senate

Hey Peter,

Early in my career, Bernie Sanders gave me a piece of advice I’ve never
forgotten.

He said, “In politics, people succeed either by being really good at lying
or really good at telling the truth. You strike me as someone who’s not
good at lying — so your job is to get really good at telling the truth.”

So today, I’m going to tell you some truths about our campaign.

We’re statistically tied with our MAGA Republican opponent. We have the
momentum to win.

December 31st marks our biggest fundraising hurdle yet — our final FEC
deadline of the year.

This is, no exaggeration, a make-or-break moment for our campaign.

In just a couple of weeks, we need to raise as much as we can to prove
that a people-powered campaign — one that rejects AIPAC and corporate PAC
money — has enough resources to take on the would-be oligarchs and win.

Finally, one more piece of truth: I don’t love asking people for money.

But because this is a 100% people-powered campaign, I’m relying on folks
like you who care about the future of our party and our country to pitch
in.

[ https://act.abdulformichigan.com/go/507?c0=em-ms-aes-20251216-1-ak.xJLQLW&amount=10&refcode=em20251216-1433&t=1004&akid=1433%2E251025%2EK6904b ]If you can contribute today, it would mean the world:

Thank you,

Abdul

Friend, This is a full-blown escalation. **The Trump administration has just sued Delaware, Maryland, New Mexico, Rhode Island, Vermont, and Washington, all in an effort to seize their voter registration rolls.** We’re talking about driver’s license numbers, Social Security numbers, full names, and home addresses for every registered voter. Secretary of State Steve Simon of Minnesota said it best: _“We’ve never seen an agency of the federal government ever demand something as broad and sweeping and with such a huge scope as this.”_ He’s right. This isn’t about “compliance.” This is about control. If the DOJ succeeds, it sets a terrifying precedent: that MAGA officials in Washington can demand any voter data, from any state, at any time, for any reason, without telling the public how it will be used or secured. We must stand with the Secretaries of State who are fighting back. If the DOJ succeeds, it sets a terrifying precedent: that MAGA officials in Washington can demand any voter data, from any state, at any time, for any reason, without telling the public how it will be used or secured. We must stand with the Secretaries of State who are fighting back. = | | | = | = | | = | = | | = | =** Thank you, Team DASS | Thank you for your dedicated support of The Democratic Association of Secretaries of State -- the only organization dedicated to electing and protecting Democratic Secretaries of States. Right now, Republicans are changing the rules and changing the players to try and change the outcomes of future elections. Democratic Secretaries of State are the last line of defense to protect our democracy and preserve the will of the voters. Please consider a donation to fight back against the GOP's voter suppression agenda. | = = We'd hate to see you go, but you can click here if you'd like to . Paid for by Democratic Association of Secretaries of State, Treasurer Denise Merrill Democratic Association of Secretaries of State 600 Pennsylvania Ave SE #15180 Washington, DC 20003 United States

name: Defund the Oligarchs via Our Revolution
subject: 71% of Americans Agree: It’s Time to Defund the Oligarchs
disclaimer_text: Paid for by Our Revolution click here to unsubscribe
body:
[ https://act.ourrevolution.com/go/25192?t=1011&refcode=email-20251216_v&akid=9978%2E5885482%2EQaNNd_ ]Our Revolution logo

Peter Willis,

A new poll confirms it: the majority of Americans recognize that
billionaires are a threat to our democracy itself.

71% of Americans want a wealth tax. A majority believe it’s time to place
real limits on how much wealth one person can amass. Even Trump’s own
pollsters were just deployed to the White House to try to sound the alarm
as his base grows restless, accusing him of putting the interests of
billionaires and tech moguls above the needs of everyday people.

[ https://act.ourrevolution.com/go/25221?source=email-20251216&t=1060&akid=9978%2E5885482%2EQaNNd_ ]Headline: Trump, 79, gave deranged response to pollster's dire warnings

Trump may refuse to listen to the warnings. But as public pressure reaches
a breaking point THIS is our moment to finally defund the oligarchs – and
we have a plan to do it. For months, we've been building a coalition –
side by side with bold members of Congress and allied organizations –
ready to hit oligarchs where it hurts most: their billions in federal
contracts.

[ https://act.ourrevolution.com/go/25192?t=1057&amount=22&amounts=9%2C22%2C27%2C46%2C66&refcode=email-20251216nd&firstname=Peter%20Willis&lastname=&email=dpwillis67%40gmail.com&zip=18326&city=Cresco&state=PA&akid=9978%2E5885482%2EQaNNd_ ]But to launch our campaign, we need your help. Can you chip in $15, $27,
or whatever you can today to fuel Our Revolution’s fight to defund the
oligarchs?

If you've stored your payment information with ActBlue Express, your
donation to Our Revolution will process immediately.

[ https://act.ourrevolution.com/go/25192?t=1042&express_lane=true&amount=9&refcode=email-20251216nd&amounts=9%2C22%2C27%2C46%2C66&firstname=Peter%20Willis&lastname=&email=dpwillis67%40gmail.com&zip=18326&city=Cresco&state=PA&akid=9978%2E5885482%2EQaNNd_ ]CHIP IN $9 [ https://act.ourrevolution.com/go/25192?t=1043&express_lane=true&amount=22&refcode=email-20251216nd&amounts=9%2C22%2C27%2C46%2C66&firstname=Peter%20Willis&lastname=&email=dpwillis67%40gmail.com&zip=18326&city=Cresco&state=PA&akid=9978%2E5885482%2EQaNNd_ ]CHIP IN $22
[ https://act.ourrevolution.com/go/25192?t=1045&express_lane=true&amount=27&refcode=email-20251216nd&amounts=9%2C22%2C27%2C46%2C66&firstname=Peter%20Willis&lastname=&email=dpwillis67%40gmail.com&zip=18326&city=Cresco&state=PA&akid=9978%2E5885482%2EQaNNd_ ]CHIP IN $27 [ https://act.ourrevolution.com/go/25192?t=1046&express_lane=true&amount=46&refcode=email-20251216nd&amounts=9%2C22%2C27%2C46%2C66&firstname=Peter%20Willis&lastname=&email=dpwillis67%40gmail.com&zip=18326&city=Cresco&state=PA&akid=9978%2E5885482%2EQaNNd_ ]CHIP IN $46
[ https://act.ourrevolution.com/go/25192?t=1047&express_lane=true&amount=66&refcode=email-20251216nd&amounts=9%2C22%2C27%2C46%2C66&firstname=Peter%20Willis&lastname=&email=dpwillis67%40gmail.com&zip=18326&city=Cresco&state=PA&akid=9978%2E5885482%2EQaNNd_ ]CHIP IN $66 [ https://act.ourrevolution.com/go/25192?t=1048&amount=9&refcode=email-20251216nd&amounts=9%2C22%2C27%2C46%2C66&firstname=Peter%20Willis&lastname=&email=dpwillis67%40gmail.com&zip=18326&city=Cresco&state=PA&akid=9978%2E5885482%2EQaNNd_ ]CHIP IN OTHER AMOUNT

Here’s the reality: our tax dollars are making billionaires richer, even
as their companies avoid paying taxes, mistreat workers, and develop
surveillance software that targets immigrants and undermines our rights.
This can’t go on.

We’re done letting federal contracts pad the pockets of Jeff Bezos, Elon
Musk, and other super-rich CEOs while families struggle to afford
groceries. Which is why on January 12, we’ll unveil our BIG PLANS to the
public. But already, all eyes are on the obscene reality: oligarchs
getting richer, enabled by our own representatives, while the rest of us
pick up the tab.

Every day the headlines get clearer – Americans are done with the myth of
“benevolent billionaires.” Even MAGA voters see the writing on the wall:
Donald Trump keeps siding with oligarchs and ignoring families’ real
needs.

We can’t let this critical moment pass. Our movement is now building real
momentum – and your support is urgent. The threat oligarchs pose to ALL of
us has become impossible to ignore. And your support right now will power
the targeted campaigns, grassroots mobilizations, and bold actions it will
take to ensure our government finally puts people over profit — STAY
TUNED!

[ https://act.ourrevolution.com/go/25192?t=1054&amount=22&refcode=email-20251216nd&amounts=9%2C22%2C27%2C46%2C66&firstname=Peter%20Willis&lastname=&email=dpwillis67%40gmail.com&zip=18326&city=Cresco&state=PA&akid=9978%2E5885482%2EQaNNd_ ]Can you rush a donation now to power a MAJOR campaign to cut off the
spigot of OUR money into billionaire bank accounts and DEFUND THE
OLIGARCHS in 2026?

When we organize, we win.

Our Revolution

name: Mark Kelly
subject: My decision:
disclaimer_text: Paid for by Maggie for NH
body:
We’ve been in some tough fights before, but this one is different. The President of the United States threatened my life, personally. I’ve got the entire executive branch bearing down on me right now.

[](https://click.ngpvan.com/k/121402706/589336326/1949054306?amount=10&refcode=LOC_EM_FR_2025.12.16_Mark-Kelly-Split_B1_F1_S1_C1_90OP-NC_RS-T0&nvep=ew0KICAiVGVuYW50VXJpIjogIm5ncHZhbjovL3Zhbi9OR1AvTkdQNDAvMS84ODg1OCIsDQogICJEaXN0cmlidXRpb25VbmlxdWVJZCI6ICJiNmU4M2JjMC1hOWRhLWYwMTEtODE5NS0wMDBkM2ExZDU4YWEiLA0KICAiRW1haWxBZGRyZXNzIjogImRwd2lsbGlzNjdAZ21haWwuY29tIg0KfQ%3D%3D&hmac=eDRMh5MgWz6zSURZ13IPB7p32Fpew4U71gcXnBDmjPo=) Hey, it’s Mark Kelly.

Seven years ago, I was making a decision about whether I was going to run for the Senate.

I knew it would be a lot of work. A lot of time away from home. A lot of time away from family. I knew it wouldn’t be easy.

But after two years of Donald Trump and a Republican-held Senate doing just about everything he wanted, I knew I couldn’t sit on the sidelines. I had to do something.

So, I ran. And, somehow, we won. And then we had to turn around and run all over again to win a full term after that. But thanks to your support, we won that one, too.

I’m grateful every day that I get to wake up, walk out the door, and defend our people, our democracy, and our Constitution. It’s one of the greatest honors of my life. And I’ve never forgotten that I wouldn’t be here without your help.

We’ve been in some tough fights before, but this one is different. The President of the United States threatened my life, personally. I’ve got the entire executive branch bearing down on me right now.

After everything you’ve already done for me, I shouldn’t have to ask you for more. But that’s what I need to do.

clean_body: We’ve been in some tough fights before, but this one is different. The President of the United States threatened my life, personally. I’ve got the entire executive branch bearing down on me right now. = Hey, it’s Mark Kelly. Seven years ago, I was making a decision about whether I was going to run for the Senate. I knew it would be a lot of work. A lot of time away from home. A lot of time away from family. I knew it wouldn’t be easy. But after two years of Donald Trump and a Republican-held Senate doing just about everything he wanted, I knew I couldn’t sit on the sidelines. I had to do something. So, I ran. And, somehow, we won. And then we had to turn around and run all over again to win a full term after that. But thanks to your support, we won that one, too. I’m grateful every day that I get to wake up, walk out the door, and defend our people, our democracy, and our Constitution. It’s one of the greatest honors of my life. And I’ve never forgotten that I wouldn’t be here without your help. We’ve been in some tough fights before, but this one is different. The President of the United States threatened my life, personally. I’ve got the entire executive branch bearing down on me right now. After everything you’ve already done for me, I shouldn’t have to ask you for more. But that’s what I need to do. = If you've saved payment information with ActBlue Express, your split donation to Mark Kelly and Maggie Hassan will go through immediately: | | = | = | = | = | = | = Thank you, Mark If you'd like to support Maggie Hassan for Senate, =. = We believe that emails are the best way for our campaign to stay in touch with supporters like you. body:
Reading is a right; banning books is un-American.
| | | | Can't see this email? [View in Your Browser](https://ctrk.klclick.com/l/01KCM59WDP7A0YT2E42CZEV767_0)

| | | [ ](https://ctrk.klclick.com/l/01KCM59WDP7A0YT2E42CZEV767_1)
---
| | | | Dear Peter,

Republican leaders continue to threaten our librarians. Recently, Tennessee’s Republican Secretary of State sent threatening letters to libraries across the state in an attempt to intimidate librarians into removing books on certain subjects and requiring them to perform subject-matter audits.
---
| | [ Help us protect books and our democracy. ](https://ctrk.klclick.com/l/01KCM59WDP7A0YT2E42CZEV767_2)
---
| This followed a Republican House member standing up in the Tennessee House and saying he would burn books that were banned from our school libraries.

Telling Tennesseans what they can and cannot read is un-American. Limiting access to information undermines key principles of our republic.

We are fighting to protect books, strengthen education and defend our librarians and educators.
---
| | [ Help us protect books and our democracy. ](https://ctrk.klclick.com/l/01KCM59WDP7A0YT2E42CZEV767_3)
---
| The Tennessee House Democratic Caucus

Did you see Ami’s latest message?

| Hi Peter,

_Did you see Ami’s latest message?_ He’s working hard in Congress to fight to protect affordable health care and extend Affordable Care Act subsidies to keep health care affordable for millions of Americans.

[**Please sign our petition today to support affordable healthcare!**](https://click.ngpvan.com/k/121403481/589338872/-495559034?source=EM_NBI_AB_20251216_EN_Healthcare_0_6MO_Bera_NA_NA_2&nvep=ew0KICAiVGVuYW50VXJpIjogIm5ncHZhbjovL3Zhbi9OR1AvTkdQMjEvMS85MDU3OCIsDQogICJEaXN0cmlidXRpb25VbmlxdWVJZCI6ICJjNGRlYzViMi1hYmRhLWYwMTEtODE5NS0wMDBkM2ExZDU4YWEiLA0KICAiRW1haWxBZGRyZXNzIjogImRwd2lsbGlzNjdAZ21haWwuY29tIg0KfQ%3D%3D&hmac=cWhYy-o7OoAlaTUK1w83xeQtXWI83SZQALJYuaR41Zc=&emci=cfdb39d3-a4d7-f011-8195-000d3a1d58aa&emdi=c4dec5b2-abda-f011-8195-000d3a1d58aa&ceid=32394541) **[SIGN ON](https://click.ngpvan.com/k/121403482/589338873/1628059965?source=EM_NBI_AB_20251216_EN_Healthcare_0_6MO_Bera_NA_NA_2&nvep=ew0KICAiVGVuYW50VXJpIjogIm5ncHZhbjovL3Zhbi9OR1AvTkdQMjEvMS85MDU3OCIsDQogICJEaXN0cmlidXRpb25VbmlxdWVJZCI6ICJjNGRlYzViMi1hYmRhLWYwMTEtODE5NS0wMDBkM2ExZDU4YWEiLA0KICAiRW1haWxBZGRyZXNzIjogImRwd2lsbGlzNjdAZ21haWwuY29tIg0KfQ%3D%3D&hmac=cWhYy-o7OoAlaTUK1w83xeQtXWI83SZQALJYuaR41Zc=&emci=cfdb39d3-a4d7-f011-8195-000d3a1d58aa&emdi=c4dec5b2-abda-f011-8195-000d3a1d58aa&ceid=32394541)**
_**Begin Forwarded Message:**_
* * *

Hi Peter,

Senate Republicans voted down a bill that would extend Affordable Care Act subsidies by three years. This bill would’ve prevented health care premiums from dramatically jumping for middle-class families, small business owners, and seniors under 65 at the start of the new year.

**The consequences of this are severe and far-reaching.

In California, over 2.3 million people are set to see soaring premiums — and here in California’s 6th District, Thisthe benchmark plan for a 60-year-old person is set to increase by 205%!**

This is a manufactured crisis and the blame lies solely with President Trump and his Republican allies in Congress.

[**Peter, f you support affordable healthcare, will you please sign onto my petition today?**](https://click.ngpvan.com/k/121403484/589338875/-495559034?source=EM_NBI_AB_20251216_EN_Healthcare_0_6MO_Bera_NA_NA_2&nvep=ew0KICAiVGVuYW50VXJpIjogIm5ncHZhbjovL3Zhbi9OR1AvTkdQMjEvMS85MDU3OCIsDQogICJEaXN0cmlidXRpb25VbmlxdWVJZCI6ICJjNGRlYzViMi1hYmRhLWYwMTEtODE5NS0wMDBkM2ExZDU4YWEiLA0KICAiRW1haWxBZGRyZXNzIjogImRwd2lsbGlzNjdAZ21haWwuY29tIg0KfQ%3D%3D&hmac=cWhYy-o7OoAlaTUK1w83xeQtXWI83SZQALJYuaR41Zc=&emci=cfdb39d3-a4d7-f011-8195-000d3a1d58aa&emdi=c4dec5b2-abda-f011-8195-000d3a1d58aa&ceid=32394541) **[ADD YOUR NAME](https://click.ngpvan.com/k/121403485/589338876/1628059965?source=EM_NBI_AB_20251216_EN_Healthcare_0_6MO_Bera_NA_NA_2&nvep=ew0KICAiVGVuYW50VXJpIjogIm5ncHZhbjovL3Zhbi9OR1AvTkdQMjEvMS85MDU3OCIsDQogICJEaXN0cmlidXRpb25VbmlxdWVJZCI6ICJjNGRlYzViMi1hYmRhLWYwMTEtODE5NS0wMDBkM2ExZDU4YWEiLA0KICAiRW1haWxBZGRyZXNzIjogImRwd2lsbGlzNjdAZ21haWwuY29tIg0KfQ%3D%3D&hmac=cWhYy-o7OoAlaTUK1w83xeQtXWI83SZQALJYuaR41Zc=&emci=cfdb39d3-a4d7-f011-8195-000d3a1d58aa&emdi=c4dec5b2-abda-f011-8195-000d3a1d58aa&ceid=32394541)**
Thanks for showing your support for this common-sense policy that helps keep health insurance affordable for millions of Americans.

Best,

Dr. Ami Bera

body:
just some friendly competition

|
---

| | [](https://click.ngpvan.com/k/121403689/589339480/-2061204679?refcode=MW_ATA_EM_FR_KaineCompetiton_251216_T1_1&nvep=ew0KICAiVGVuYW50VXJpIjogIm5ncHZhbjovL3Zhbi9OR1AvTkdQNDAvMS84ODY1NCIsDQogICJEaXN0cmlidXRpb25VbmlxdWVJZCI6ICIxNzVhYjc2Zi1hY2RhLWYwMTEtODE5NS0wMDBkM2ExZDU4YWEiLA0KICAiRW1haWxBZGRyZXNzIjogImRwd2lsbGlzNjdAZ21haWwuY29tIg0KfQ%3D%3D&hmac=ImQp4iF2prEtg-1Eicpx6sVkoaBICKDGzpw6-22pm1s=) You know, Senator Warner and I go way back.

We share the same vision: **to make Virginia the best place to start a family, get an education, and build a business.** [](https://click.ngpvan.com/k/121403690/589339481/-2061204679?refcode=MW_ATA_EM_FR_KaineCompetiton_251216_T1_1&nvep=ew0KICAiVGVuYW50VXJpIjogIm5ncHZhbjovL3Zhbi9OR1AvTkdQNDAvMS84ODY1NCIsDQogICJEaXN0cmlidXRpb25VbmlxdWVJZCI6ICIxNzVhYjc2Zi1hY2RhLWYwMTEtODE5NS0wMDBkM2ExZDU4YWEiLA0KICAiRW1haWxBZGRyZXNzIjogImRwd2lsbGlzNjdAZ21haWwuY29tIg0KfQ%3D%3D&hmac=ImQp4iF2prEtg-1Eicpx6sVkoaBICKDGzpw6-22pm1s=) So when I heard he was falling behind on his online fundraising goal for the month (his most important deadline of the year!), I told him I’d lend a hand.

In fact, I made a friendly bet with him. I wagered that I could recruit more grassroots donors to contribute to my email than he could.

So if you (like I do) want to see Senator Warner humbled by Virginia’s _junior_ Senator, you can do so by throwing a few bucks to this ActBlue page:

[**_https://secure.actblue.com/donate/mw-email-251216-kainecompetition_**](https://click.ngpvan.com/k/121403691/589339482/-2061204679?refcode=MW_ATA_EM_FR_KaineCompetiton_251216_T1_1&nvep=ew0KICAiVGVuYW50VXJpIjogIm5ncHZhbjovL3Zhbi9OR1AvTkdQNDAvMS84ODY1NCIsDQogICJEaXN0cmlidXRpb25VbmlxdWVJZCI6ICIxNzVhYjc2Zi1hY2RhLWYwMTEtODE5NS0wMDBkM2ExZDU4YWEiLA0KICAiRW1haWxBZGRyZXNzIjogImRwd2lsbGlzNjdAZ21haWwuY29tIg0KfQ%3D%3D&hmac=ImQp4iF2prEtg-1Eicpx6sVkoaBICKDGzpw6-22pm1s=)

In all seriousness, your donation today will go to a very important goal: reelecting a fierce advocate for Virginian families, a consensus-builder in an increasingly partisan Senate, and a guy who has played an important role in fighting back against Donald Trump’s worst impulses.

And as an added bonus, when you donate today, you’ll help me win a friendly bet against him. It’s really a win-win-win. **[DONATE](https://click.ngpvan.com/k/121403692/589339483/-2061204679?refcode=MW_ATA_EM_FR_KaineCompetiton_251216_T1_1&nvep=ew0KICAiVGVuYW50VXJpIjogIm5ncHZhbjovL3Zhbi9OR1AvTkdQNDAvMS84ODY1NCIsDQogICJEaXN0cmlidXRpb25VbmlxdWVJZCI6ICIxNzVhYjc2Zi1hY2RhLWYwMTEtODE5NS0wMDBkM2ExZDU4YWEiLA0KICAiRW1haWxBZGRyZXNzIjogImRwd2lsbGlzNjdAZ21haWwuY29tIg0KfQ%3D%3D&hmac=ImQp4iF2prEtg-1Eicpx6sVkoaBICKDGzpw6-22pm1s=)** Thanks a bunch,

Tim Kaine **[DONATE](https://click.ngpvan.com/k/121403693/589339484/-2061204679?refcode=MW_ATA_EM_FR_KaineCompetiton_251216_T1_1&nvep=ew0KICAiVGVuYW50VXJpIjogIm5ncHZhbjovL3Zhbi9OR1AvTkdQNDAvMS84ODY1NCIsDQogICJEaXN0cmlidXRpb25VbmlxdWVJZCI6ICIxNzVhYjc2Zi1hY2RhLWYwMTEtODE5NS0wMDBkM2ExZDU4YWEiLA0KICAiRW1haWxBZGRyZXNzIjogImRwd2lsbGlzNjdAZ21haWwuY29tIg0KfQ%3D%3D&hmac=ImQp4iF2prEtg-1Eicpx6sVkoaBICKDGzpw6-22pm1s=)**

name: Oregon Department of Justice
subject: Update from AG Dan Rayfield
body:
ICE, public safety, Dec. 17 Town Hall, and DOJ wrapped
---
| | | |
---
|
# The Oregon Department of Justice is working every day on behalf of Oregonians.
The year is coming to a close - yet our work at DOJ is not slowing down. We have over 1,600 public servants working every day to uphold Oregon's values, keep our communities safe, and protect seniors, consumers, and working families: recently, we secured a $700 million settlement with Google for consumers, protected disaster preparedness funding for communities from Astoria to Grants Pass, and returned a vital search-and-rescue helicopter to Newport. Scroll down for even more:
* Responding to 1000+ emails, calls, and letters about ICE
* A new public safety pilot program: SPIRE
* Town hall in SW Portland on Dec. 17
* DOJ wrapped: Highlights from 2025

Wishing you and yours a warm and cozy season, Dan Rayfield, Oregon Attorney General
---
| |
* * *
---
|
# Responding to your concerns about ICE
Over one thousand Oregonians have reached out to our office with concerns about ICE in Oregon communities - and that's just in the past few weeks. We're using all the tools available here at the Oregon DOJ, and we've also put together resources to help, and to report federal misconduct. [Watch to learn how we're responding to ICE.](https://links-2.govdelivery.com/CL0/https:%2F%2Fbsky.app%2Fprofile%2Fagdanrayfield.bsky.social%2Fpost%2F3m7ska5gojc23/1/0101019b286f23df-c94c8e37-a1b8-48ac-ba9c-1eae4c93fc60-000000/WukyV3eE2T8XmAoSrotTSdcQ4-YBQ8k4agyJqq7aIRU=435)
---
[](https://links-2.govdelivery.com/CL0/https:%2F%2Fbsky.app%2Fprofile%2Fagdanrayfield.bsky.social%2Fpost%2F3m7ska5gojc23/2/0101019b286f23df-c94c8e37-a1b8-48ac-ba9c-1eae4c93fc60-000000/-sAFRsYnia7VRZSP2TCClubTM2otUmZeaV7MO17JmuY=435)
---
| |
* * *
---
| | [](https://links-2.govdelivery.com/CL0/https:%2F%2Fwww.doj.state.or.us%2Fmedia-home%2Fnews-media-releases%2Fag-rayfield-and-wash-county-da-barton-announce-program-to-tackle-crime%2F/1/0101019b286f23df-c94c8e37-a1b8-48ac-ba9c-1eae4c93fc60-000000/3iHnGvEprPOKZIp4R0MjAXWUM3ApQ7syK9DNu3F_3Tk=435)
---
# A new public safety pilot program: SPIRE
One of my top priorities as Attorney General is keeping communities safe. We recently announced SPIRE, a new pilot program to tackle organized crime and help keep our communities safe through collaboration and resource-sharing with local law enforcement. SPIRE—Special Projects: Investigate, Respond, Enforce—grew out of conversations across Oregon about the kinds of crime our communities are facing. We’re partnering with Washington County DA Kevin Barton for this pilot. [Learn more about how we're keeping communities safe.](https://links-2.govdelivery.com/CL0/https:%2F%2Fwww.doj.state.or.us%2Fmedia-home%2Fnews-media-releases%2Fag-rayfield-and-wash-county-da-barton-announce-program-to-tackle-crime%2F/2/0101019b286f23df-c94c8e37-a1b8-48ac-ba9c-1eae4c93fc60-000000/QSqoNBDfkMZuvDxz4YbH0egkFm_6AJy79ZEgPo3nyiQ=435).
---
| |
* * *
---
|
# Town Hall in SW Portland on December 17
Join us on Wednesday, December 17 for our next Oregon Priorities Town Hall. We'll be joined by Senate President Rob Wagner at PCC Sylvania. As the Oregon DOJ works to keep communities safe, protect consumers, seniors, working families and defend Oregonians values, we want to hear what’s most important to you and your family. ASL and Spanish interpretation provided. [Click here to RSVP, sign up to give public comment, and/or submit a question in advance. ](https://links-2.govdelivery.com/CL0/http:%2F%2Fbit.ly%2FDec17AG/1/0101019b286f23df-c94c8e37-a1b8-48ac-ba9c-1eae4c93fc60-000000/TxPD9ND2tyxfAijuBcz3wqGdd_y-mcrkMtrpwAPA_4I=435)
---
[](https://links-2.govdelivery.com/CL0/https:%2F%2Fforms.office.com%2FPages%2FResponsePage.aspx%3Fid=R5oHZGLPEk2_1R5x3945wlHVbZlv4G9PnGU0a9dd6XlUNkwySVVBT1M1WU0yVEg3VzVRSk0zVVE0Wi4u/1/0101019b286f23df-c94c8e37-a1b8-48ac-ba9c-1eae4c93fc60-000000/J6EE8XfZktFQN-USCaWj24nrrRfGzbeWvPfRl5T2VAc=435)
---
| |
* * *
---
| [](https://links-2.govdelivery.com/CL0/https:%2F%2Fbsky.app%2Fprofile%2Fagdanrayfield.bsky.social%2Fpost%2F3m6pmjxgpu222/1/0101019b286f23df-c94c8e37-a1b8-48ac-ba9c-1eae4c93fc60-000000/lZbb4GQGmkAmAKmDLy8UhwaP2NCM3FfK7XYTrf6-_dw=435)
---
# Stay safe from scams this holiday season
It's important to learn how to recognize scams. Scammers are getting more sophisticated, and the best way to keep yourself safe is to learn the warning signs - and share them with family and friends.

The Oregon DOJ has a Consumer Hotline you can call anytime with questions: 877-877-9392. [Learn more about how to avoid scams on our website.](https://links-2.govdelivery.com/CL0/https:%2F%2Fwww.doj.state.or.us%2Fconsumer-protection%2F/1/0101019b286f23df-c94c8e37-a1b8-48ac-ba9c-1eae4c93fc60-000000/juyZgUwc2I61AxCYkuudJ1_6TYs5Sg-kb1qq4kc9_Wg=435)
---
| |
* * *
---
|
# Defending Oregon's values
The Oregon Department of Justice is working every day to defend Oregon's values - including protecting Oregon from federal overreach. To date, we have safeguarded **over $4.5 billion in federal dollars** coming to our state through our lawsuits. Learn more about [funds we've protected](https://links-2.govdelivery.com/CL0/https:%2F%2Fwww.doj.state.or.us%2Foregon-department-of-justice%2Ffederal-oversight%2Ffederal-money-safeguarded-for-oregon-to-date%2F/1/0101019b286f23df-c94c8e37-a1b8-48ac-ba9c-1eae4c93fc60-000000/gh-hpcWNuqjUnprs2Y9X4FKfHIRu4NMI08RqLvVGV5c=435). Track specific lawsuits in our [federal litigation tracker](https://links-2.govdelivery.com/CL0/https:%2F%2Fwww.doj.state.or.us%2Foregon-department-of-justice%2Ffederal-oversight%2Ffederal-litigation-tracker%2F/1/0101019b286f23df-c94c8e37-a1b8-48ac-ba9c-1eae4c93fc60-000000/0bRaXtUlkn2i03XOhRC29nNjDAZl_FB7Ak6WndzNSBA=435).
---
[](https://links-2.govdelivery.com/CL0/https:%2F%2Fwww.doj.state.or.us%2Foregon-department-of-justice%2Ffederal-oversight%2Ffederal-money-safeguarded-for-oregon-to-date%2F/2/0101019b286f23df-c94c8e37-a1b8-48ac-ba9c-1eae4c93fc60-000000/sfRMy0oWv4aKU1WRfcnC1iGHkjx63Yxy_8Z7pY3euPk=435)
---
| |
* * *
---
| [](https://links-2.govdelivery.com/CL0/https:%2F%2Fbsky.app%2Fprofile%2Fagdanrayfield.bsky.social%2Fpost%2F3ma4jgdz2is27/1/0101019b286f23df-c94c8e37-a1b8-48ac-ba9c-1eae4c93fc60-000000/ISh6V-Z6koKAenAQS9H3XVQnx7wIr7L6Z3eT7z2rn48=435)
---
# DOJ wrapped: Highlights from 2025
Our team put together "Oregon DOJ wrapped" to highlight some key stats from our office this year - including receiving over 31,500 emails, phone calls, and letters from you, securing over $77 million in settlements for Oregon, and welcoming over 1,450 people to our town halls. [See the full recap here.](https://links-2.govdelivery.com/CL0/https:%2F%2Fbsky.app%2Fprofile%2Fagdanrayfield.bsky.social%2Fpost%2F3ma4jgdz2is27/2/0101019b286f23df-c94c8e37-a1b8-48ac-ba9c-1eae4c93fc60-000000/RrSTd2OQQbQeZBhujciuZov7sHsdVLr2Xr3bxLocHdY=435)
---
| |
* * *
---
|
# We want to hear from you!
Email us at [AttorneyGeneral@doj.oregon.gov](mailto:AttorneyGeneral@doj.oregon.gov) or call **503-378-6002** with questions, comments, or concerns. You can also stay in touch on social media:

clean_body: ICE, public safety, Dec. 17 Town Hall, and DOJ wrapped | | | | | The Oregon Department of Justice is working every day on behalf of Oregonians. The year is coming to a close - yet our work at DOJ is not slowing down. We have over 1,600 public servants working every day to uphold Oregon's values, keep our communities safe, and protect seniors, consumers, and working families: recently, we secured a $700 million settlement with Google for consumers, protected disaster preparedness funding for communities from Astoria to Grants Pass, and returned a vital search-and-rescue helicopter to Newport. Scroll down for even more: Responding to 1000+ emails, calls, and letters about ICE A new public safety pilot program: SPIRE Town hall in SW Portland on Dec. 17 DOJ wrapped: Highlights from 2025 Wishing you and yours a warm and cozy season, Dan Rayfield, Oregon Attorney General | | | Responding to your concerns about ICE Over one thousand Oregonians have reached out to our office with concerns about ICE in Oregon communities - and that's just in the past few weeks. We're using all the tools available here at the Oregon DOJ, and we've also put together resources to help, and to report federal misconduct. | | | | A new public safety pilot program: SPIRE One of my top priorities as Attorney General is keeping communities safe. We recently announced SPIRE, a new pilot program to tackle organized crime and help keep our communities safe through collaboration and resource-sharing with local law enforcement. SPIRE—Special Projects: Investigate, Respond, Enforce—grew out of conversations across Oregon about the kinds of crime our communities are facing. We’re partnering with Washington County DA Kevin Barton for this pilot. . | | | Town Hall in SW Portland on December 17 Join us on Wednesday, December 17 for our next Oregon Priorities Town Hall. We'll be joined by Senate President Rob Wagner at PCC Sylvania. As the Oregon DOJ works to keep communities safe, protect consumers, seniors, working families and defend Oregonians values, we want to hear what’s most important to you and your family. ASL and Spanish interpretation provided. | | | Stay safe from scams this holiday season It's important to learn how to recognize scams. Scammers are getting more sophisticated, and the best way to keep yourself safe is to learn the warning signs - and share them with family and friends. The Oregon DOJ has a Consumer Hotline you can call anytime with questions: 877-877-9392. | | | Defending Oregon's values The Oregon Department of Justice is working every day to defend Oregon's values - including protecting Oregon from federal overreach. To date, we have safeguarded over $4.5 billion in federal dollars coming to our state through our lawsuits. Learn more about . Track specific lawsuits in our . | | | DOJ wrapped: Highlights from 2025 Our team put together "Oregon DOJ wrapped" to highlight some key stats from our office this year - including receiving over 31,500 emails, phone calls, and letters from you, securing over $77 million in settlements for Oregon, and welcoming over 1,450 people to our town halls. | | | We want to hear from you! Email us at mailto:AttorneyGeneral@doj.oregon.gov or call 503-378-6002 with questions, comments, or concerns. You can also stay in touch on social media: If someone forwarded you this email, you can . | | | | Update your subscriptions, modify your password or email address, or stop subscriptions at any time on your . You will need to use your email address to log in. If you have questions or problems with the subscription service, please visit . This service is provided to you at no charge by . | *

name: Kevin Kijewski for Michigan Attorney General
subject: Wow. A Judge Praised a Violent Criminal
disclaimer_text: Paid for by Kevin Kijewski for Michigan, 115 W Allegan St, Ste 900, Lansing, MI 48933_
body:
| | | | | | | [View this email in your browser](https://mailchi.mp/kevinformi/wow-a-judge-praised-a-violent-criminal-4741155?e=4b10910eed)
---
[|
---](https://kevinformi.us3.list-manage.com/track/click?u=39782b0ca6b504d2faad7a196&id=eeb172845a&e=4b10910eed)
| | | | | |
# What happened in that Michigan courtroom is beyond outrageous
---
| | Dear Friend and Patriot,
What happened in that Michigan courtroom is beyond outrageous: it’s dangerous.
An Obama-appointed federal judge praised an illegal immigrant who raped and sodomized a woman with cerebral palsy... and then refused to add just two more years to his sentence for repeatedly entering our country illegally.
She called him an “ambassador for living up to our immigration restrictions.”
**An ambassador!**
This monster brutalized a disabled woman, entered the United States at least seven times, and was rewarded with compliments from the bench instead of the full measure of justice. And now he’ll be back on the streets years earlier than he should be b**ecause a radical judge decided her ideology mattered more than the victim**.
I spoke out because this isn’t justice. It’s judicial activism that prioritizes criminals over citizens and spits on the work of federal law enforcement under President Trump.
**This is exactly why I’m running for Attorney General.**
Michigan needs an AG who stands firmly with victims, works with ICE to remove violent offenders from our communities, and ends the lenient, reckless treatment of lawbreakers that has turned our state into a haven for lawlessness. I will do that job on day one.
But I can’t do it alone.
The left is already celebrating decisions like this — and they will pour everything they have into keeping a weak, ideological Attorney General in office. If we’re going to take back Michigan and restore real justice, we need to build a winning operation now.
I’m asking you to step up today:
**Chip in $25, $50, $100, or more to help me fight for victims and take back the Attorney General’s office.**
---
| | | | | | | | | | [|
---](https://kevinformi.us3.list-manage.com/track/click?u=39782b0ca6b504d2faad7a196&id=0f5faa1d1c&e=4b10910eed)
| | Michigan families deserve safety. Victims deserve justice. Criminals deserve punishment... not praise.
Next year, we fix this. But only if we have the resources to win.
Thank you for standing with me, Kevin Kijewski
Candidate for Michigan Attorney GeneralP.S. This election will determine whether violent offenders get sympathy from the bench... or consequences. If you’re with me, please donate today.
---
| | | | | [](https://kevinformi.us3.list-manage.com/track/click?u=39782b0ca6b504d2faad7a196&id=69bf14c5f1&e=4b10910eed)

name: Gabby Giffords
subject: Sandy Hook Elementary School
disclaimer_text: Paid For By Giffords PAC; not authorized by any candidate or candidate's committee
body:
It's just impossible to comprehend.
#

Friends -
This week marked 13 years since the tragedy at Sandy Hook Elementary School in Newtown, Connecticut.
On this devastating day, 20 innocent children’s lives were stolen—all of them just six or seven years old. Six school staff members went to work that morning and never returned home.
I want to take a moment to ask you to join me in keeping the families impacted by this tragedy in your hearts. No matter how many years pass, it will never be possible to wrap our minds around the scene in Newtown's firehouse when 26 families were told by Connecticut's governor that if they hadn't yet been reunited with their loved ones, it wasn't going to happen.
It's just impossible to comprehend.
I’ll never forget when Mark and I traveled to Newtown shortly after the tragedy to spend time with grieving families.
We gathered in a living room filled with parents of some of the children killed in their classrooms, as well as family members of some of the school staff members who lost their lives.
After talking to every family there that day and tearing up at every picture of the innocent victims, I remember walking to a chair in the corner, collapsing into it and saying, "Enough."
A few short weeks later, we launched our gun violence prevention organization, then known as Americans for Responsible Solutions. Back then, I could never have guessed that we would build a coalition of millions of people—just like you—committed to our mission to save lives from gun violence.
In the 13 years since the Sandy Hook tragedy, I can tell you confidently that the work we’ve done has changed hearts, minds, and laws.
Make no mistake: the fight isn’t over yet, and the headwinds we face are stiff. But if we all keep working together, I know that someday, there will be a future where we no longer live under the constant threat of gun violence.
With courage,
Gabby Giffords

**Please consider making a contribution of $5, $10 or anything you can afford to GIFFORDS PAC today. Especially today. It all adds up when we’re all chipping in and that’s how we’ll create the change we need on gun safety...together.**
**[DONATE](https://go.giffords.org/go/14457?c0=giffords-em-q42025.UDsFRy&refcode=em251216-1-b&t=1&akid=11104%2E3935704%2EuGAiWK)**

Paid For By Giffords PAC; not authorized by any candidate or candidate's committee. www.Giffords.org
This email was sent to dpwillis67@gmail.com. We try to send only the most important information and opportunities to participate via email, but click [here](https://go.giffords.org/unsubscribe/unsubscribe/?t=2&akid=11104%2E3935704%2EuGAiWK) if you'd like to unsubscribe. Giffords PAC was started to support elected officials who step up to fight the gun violence epidemic. Thank you for standing with Gabby and the Giffords team.

name: Autism Health
subject: Adopt-A-Family for the Holidays!
body:
Adopt-A-Family for the Holidays!

Support Families. Restore Hope. Change Lives.

This holiday season, Autism Health is launching our Adopt-A-Family initiative to help children and adults with autism access care that can truly change the trajectory of their lives. Many families in our community are struggling... financially, emotionally, and physically. But too often, the resources they need are out of reach.

To meet this need, we have partnered with Dr. Goodenowe Enterprises and the Dr. Goodenowe Community Health Initiative to provide families with a comprehensive support system that includes plasmalogen precursor supplementation, community education, monthly mentorship, and guidance from trained navigators who walk alongside families every step of the way.

These programs are already helping individuals experience improvements in focus, communication, sleep, mood, anxiety levels, and overall daily functioning. When you adopt a family for the holidays, you are directly funding access to this full restorative support system.

How You Can Help

By adopting a family, you provide three months or more of life-changing support, including:

• Plasmalogen precursor supplements

• Access to the Engage community platform

• Monthly mentorship through the Community Health Navigator program

• Education, guidance, and community connection

Whether you support one individual or multiple families, your contribution will bring hope and stability to those who need it most this holiday season.
DONATE HERE (https://givebutter.com/goodenowe?gbtid=0df4a7de-f90d-4655-84b4-4919742c4d3b&mc_cid=f3cec25dab&mc_eid=UNIQID)

** Autism Health Summit 2026 – Last Month for Discounted Tickets
------------------------------------------------------------

The countdown is on! This is the final month to purchase your tickets to the Autism Health Summit 2026 at the discounted rate. Beginning January 1st, ticket prices will increase by $50, so now is the perfect time to secure your spot.

For this month only, you can take an additional $25 off your ticket with promo code Holiday25 at checkout.

Join us April 24–26, 2026, at the beautiful Legacy Center Resort & Spa in San Diego, California, for a powerful three-day event that brings together leading voices in advocacy, research, and integrative health.

Our headline speakers include:

• Aaron Siri, Esq. – Medical freedom attorney

• Del Bigtree – Investigative journalist and host of The HighWire

• Dr. Toby Rogers – Researcher and public health analyst

View the agenda HERE (https://track.pstmrk.it/3s/ahsconference.com%2Fagenda%2F/b43o/kRTCAQ/AQ/f167c061-9032-4d8c-85d5-9c64a3b351c9/2/zoFLLVZCR7?mc_cid=f3cec25dab&mc_eid=UNIQID) .

Seats are limited, and the Summit is expected to sell out. Reserve your spot today and join a transformative weekend of education, empowerment, and community.
REGISTER HERE (https://ahsconference.com/pricing-table/?mc_cid=f3cec25dab&mc_eid=UNIQID)

About Autism Health Inc.
Autism Health is a 501(c)(3) nonprofit organization dedicated to educating individuals, families, and communities about autism spectrum disorder (ASD). Autism Health provides educational programs, workshops, events, research, and resources tailored to meet the diverse needs of those impacted by autism and other neurological disorders.
DONATE HERE (https://autismhealth.com/donations/?mc_cid=f3cec25dab&mc_eid=UNIQID)
Autism Health Inc. depends on generous donations from our community. Large or small, every donation gets us closer to achieving our goals. We are grateful for your assistance! For more information about Autism Health, visit www.AutismHealth.com
(https://track.pstmrk.it/3s/track.pstmrk.it%2F3s%2Ftrack.pstmrk.it%252F3s%252Ftrack.pstmrk.it%25252F3s%25252Ftrack.pstmrk.it%2525252F3s%2525252Ftrack.pstmrk.it%252525252F3s%252525252Ftrack.pstmrk.it%25252525252F3s%25252525252Ftrack.pstmrk.it%2525252525252F3s%2525252525252Ftrack.pstmrk.it%252525252525252F3s%252525252525252Ftrack.pstmrk.it%25252525252525252F3s%25252525252525252Ftrack.pstmrk.it%2525252525252525252F3s%2525252525252525252Ftrack.pstmrk.it%252525252525252525252F3s%252525252525252525252Ftrack.pstmrk.it%25252525252525252525252F3s%25252525252525252525252Fwww.AutismHealth.com%25252525252525252525252Fb43o%25252525252525252525252FD_6-AQ%25252525252525252525252FAQ%25252525252525252525252Fa5b69cac-ff7c-4ec9-aac6-44b40fec951c%25252525252525252525252F7%25252525252525252525252FoVEfE-VS5D%252525252525252525252Fb43o%252525252525252525252FxRTAAQ%252525252525252525252FAQ%252525252525252525252Fc672640d-97e5-43e3-8630-7c85d7d3632b%252525252525252525252F7%252525252525252525252FLYuCcxT524%2525252525?mc_cid=f3cec25dab&mc_eid=UNIQID

# Hello Capitalists,
### Here is everything you should be following today:
* Trump launches "Tech Force" to spearhead US AI adoption
* Partners with Google, Nvidia, OpenAI, Oracle, Palantir and Salesforce
* AI stocks hit the skids as financing worries mount
* The Pentagon is now in the Zinc business
* Zillow is about to be in the fight of it's life
* Oil crashes to 7 year low, gas prices follow
* OpenAI just poached top talent from Google to join its growing roster

### **Today 's markets + assets:**
* **🔴 DOW: 48096.54 (⬇️ 0.66%)**
* **🔴 S&P: 6781.29 (⬇️ 0.52%)**
* **🔴 NASDAQ: 23012.40 (⬇️ 0.20%)**
* **⚠ ️✅CBOE VIX Volatility Index: 16.77 (⬆️ 1.64%)**
* **✅ Gold: $4336.80 (⬆️ 0.05%)**
* **🔴 Silver: $63.52 (⬇️ 0.07%)**
* **✅ Bitcoin: $87,826 (⬆️ 2.40%)**

* * *
* * *
## The Capitalist is a reader-supported publication Reject Corporate Left-Wing Journalism
[Support What We Do](https://substack.com/redirect/a1eb8160-eb32-40ea-8882-b633be698a67?j=eyJ1IjoiMWh2cjYwIn0.VBabVq-VHATXCJqsddI7uMpdiTm0WkVanRDZfvCWMkg)
* * *
* * *
# Trump launches AI "Tech Force" in partnership with America's Tech Titans
The Trump administration on Monday **[unveiled the"U.S. Tech Force,"](https://substack.com/redirect/84f28be8-24b7-4243-8b7d-facef9b41cf7?j=eyJ1IjoiMWh2cjYwIn0.VBabVq-VHATXCJqsddI7uMpdiTm0WkVanRDZfvCWMkg)** a new initiative to recruit about 1,000 engineers and specialists for two-year stints building AI infrastructure across federal agencies, in partnership with tech giants including Amazon Web Services, Apple and Microsoft, amid intensifying U.S.-China competition.
| [](https://substack.com/redirect/84f28be8-24b7-4243-8b7d-facef9b41cf7?j=eyJ1IjoiMWh2cjYwIn0.VBabVq-VHATXCJqsddI7uMpdiTm0WkVanRDZfvCWMkg)|
---|---|---
* **Two-Year Specialist Commitment:** Participants commit to two years working on high-impact projects like AI implementation, data modernization and digital services, reporting directly to agency leaders.
* **Tech Giants Partner Deeply:** Partners including Google, Nvidia, OpenAI, Oracle, Palantir and Salesforce will nominate employees for service and prioritize hiring program alumni afterward.
* **Competitive Salaries Offered:** Roles feature annual pay estimated at $150,000 to $200,000 plus benefits, targeting early-career technologists to address federal talent gaps.
* **Boosts U.S. AI Dominance:** The program advances Trump's push for national AI leadership, following his recent executive order establishing a unified federal policy framework over state regulations.

* * *
* * *
**[Together with Ifrah Law](https://substack.com/redirect/99de242b-9303-4644-8ea6-9a7964c5f59f?j=eyJ1IjoiMWh2cjYwIn0.VBabVq-VHATXCJqsddI7uMpdiTm0WkVanRDZfvCWMkg)**
# **Ifrah Law heightens the success of iGaming clients through every phase of their business cycle**
Led by a managing partner who has been **[at the center of every significant court case affecting online gaming,](https://substack.com/redirect/99de242b-9303-4644-8ea6-9a7964c5f59f?j=eyJ1IjoiMWh2cjYwIn0.VBabVq-VHATXCJqsddI7uMpdiTm0WkVanRDZfvCWMkg)** Ifrah Law operates at the cutting edge of technology, innovation, and regulation.
Its lawyers represent industry players throughout the entire business cycle, from the formation of a corporation or licensing relationship, through marketing, partnering, growth, and disputes, to profitable exits.
We're proud to share that all partners at Ifrah Law have been ranked by Chambers and Partners, the gold standard for evaluating legal excellence worldwide.
| [](https://substack.com/redirect/99de242b-9303-4644-8ea6-9a7964c5f59f?j=eyJ1IjoiMWh2cjYwIn0.VBabVq-VHATXCJqsddI7uMpdiTm0WkVanRDZfvCWMkg)|
---|---|---
[Learn More](https://substack.com/redirect/99de242b-9303-4644-8ea6-9a7964c5f59f?j=eyJ1IjoiMWh2cjYwIn0.VBabVq-VHATXCJqsddI7uMpdiTm0WkVanRDZfvCWMkg)
* * *
* * *
# AI infrastructure stocks plunge**over financing worries**
The AI infrastructure **[selloff deepened on Wall Street Monday,](https://substack.com/redirect/eee0a2df-c65f-4b94-b06b-0f17f52e515f?j=eyJ1IjoiMWh2cjYwIn0.VBabVq-VHATXCJqsddI7uMpdiTm0WkVanRDZfvCWMkg)** with shares of Broadcom tumbling 5.6%, Oracle sliding 2.7%, and CoreWeave dropping about 8% amid mounting investor worries over financing massive data center expansions.
| [](https://substack.com/redirect/eee0a2df-c65f-4b94-b06b-0f17f52e515f?j=eyJ1IjoiMWh2cjYwIn0.VBabVq-VHATXCJqsddI7uMpdiTm0WkVanRDZfvCWMkg)|
---|---|---
* **Broadcom Faces Margin Pressure** : Despite forecasting AI chip sales doubling to $8.2 billion, lower gross margins on AI systems contributed to an 11% drop last week and further declines.
* **Oracle 's Debt Burden Soars**: Capital expenditures jumped to $50 billion with lease commitments up 148% to $248 billion, fueling concerns over its 500% debt-to-equity ratio versus peers.
* **CoreWeave Hits Debt Risks** : The Nvidia GPU cloud provider, down over 60% from its June high, carries a 120% debt-to-equity ratio, amplifying selloff pressures.
* **Investors Demand Proven ROI** : Market skepticism is growing on funding sustainability, even as AI demand surges and experts note potential revenue from more compute capacity.

* * *
* * *
# Pentagon grabs stake in massive Tennessee smelter
The Pentagon is acquiring **[a 40% stake in a $7.4 billion critical minerals smelter](https://substack.com/redirect/e3969164-11b2-4c45-832e-056714c7896d?j=eyJ1IjoiMWh2cjYwIn0.VBabVq-VHATXCJqsddI7uMpdiTm0WkVanRDZfvCWMkg)** in Tennessee through a partnership with South Korea's Korea Zinc, aiming to produce 540,000 tons annually and reduce U.S. reliance on China-dominated supply chains.
| [](https://substack.com/redirect/e3969164-11b2-4c45-832e-056714c7896d?j=eyJ1IjoiMWh2cjYwIn0.VBabVq-VHATXCJqsddI7uMpdiTm0WkVanRDZfvCWMkg)|
---|---|---
* **Share Sale Details** : Korea Zinc will issue $1.9 billion in new shares to a U.S. government-controlled joint venture, giving it and strategic investors 10% ownership in the company.
* **Priority Access Granted** : The agreement ensures the United States gains priority access to Korea Zinc's expanded global critical minerals production starting in 2026.
* **National Security Boost** : Officials emphasize the deal strengthens defense, semiconductors, AI, and advanced manufacturing by rebuilding domestic processing absent since the 1970s.
* **Broader Trump Strategy** : This follows similar Pentagon investments, including a major stake in rare earth miner MP Materials and an $8.5 billion framework with Australia.

* * *
* * *
# Google's real estate move could spell doom for Zillow
Zillow shares **[plunged more than 9% Monday,](https://substack.com/redirect/1efc0c4c-3f60-4d46-a07e-90e8221e8e9e?j=eyJ1IjoiMWh2cjYwIn0.VBabVq-VHATXCJqsddI7uMpdiTm0WkVanRDZfvCWMkg)**[ ](https://substack.com/redirect/1efc0c4c-3f60-4d46-a07e-90e8221e8e9e?j=eyJ1IjoiMWh2cjYwIn0.VBabVq-VHATXCJqsddI7uMpdiTm0WkVanRDZfvCWMkg)wiping out about $1.6 billion in market value, after Google began testing real estate listings directly in its search results--sparking fears of heightened long-term competition in the online property portal space.
| [](https://substack.com/redirect/1efc0c4c-3f60-4d46-a07e-90e8221e8e9e?j=eyJ1IjoiMWh2cjYwIn0.VBabVq-VHATXCJqsddI7uMpdiTm0WkVanRDZfvCWMkg)|
---|---|---
* **Google Tests Listings** : Google is experimenting with for-sale property listings in select markets on mobile, powered by HouseCanary, enabling users to view details, request tours, and contact agents without leaving search.
* **Limited Near-Term Impact** : Analysts expect minimal immediate effects, as most of Zillow's traffic (228 million users in Q3) comes directly via its app and site rather than organic search.
* **Potential Long-Term Risks** : A nationwide rollout could gradually erode portal traffic, raise acquisition costs, and pressure lead generation and monetization over years.
* **Analyst Views Mixed** : Goldman Sachs sees Google's move as a long-term headwind; Wells Fargo anticipates higher traffic expenses but no disintermediation; Oppenheimer warns of multi-year implications if expanded.

* * *
* * *
# Oil prices plunge to 7-year low
Crude oil futures[ ](https://substack.com/redirect/810f1a63-e47c-4579-821f-9a74a325e36c?j=eyJ1IjoiMWh2cjYwIn0.VBabVq-VHATXCJqsddI7uMpdiTm0WkVanRDZfvCWMkg)**[tumbled Tuesday to their lowest levels since May,](https://substack.com/redirect/810f1a63-e47c-4579-821f-9a74a325e36c?j=eyJ1IjoiMWh2cjYwIn0.VBabVq-VHATXCJqsddI7uMpdiTm0WkVanRDZfvCWMkg)** with U.S. benchmark WTI dropping 2.13% to $55.61 per barrel and Brent falling 1.93% to $59.39, amid surging OPEC+ supply and fading Ukraine war risks.
| [](https://substack.com/redirect/810f1a63-e47c-4579-821f-9a74a325e36c?j=eyJ1IjoiMWh2cjYwIn0.VBabVq-VHATXCJqsddI7uMpdiTm0WkVanRDZfvCWMkg)|
---|---|---
* **OPEC+ Boosts Production Rapidly** : After years of output cuts, the group has ramped up supply, creating a market surplus that's driving prices lower this year.
* **Geopolitical Tensions Ease Significantly** : Prospects of a Ukraine-Russia peace deal under Trump administration pressure has reduced threats of supply disruptions that have plagued the market since since the 2022 invasion.
* **Worst Annual Drop Looming** : WTI down 22% year-to-date (steepest since 2018), while Brent has fallen nearly 20% (worst since 2020).
* **U.S. Gasoline Prices Collapse** : Average pump prices dipped below $3 per gallon nationwide, hitting the lowest level in four years, per AAA data.

* * *
* * *
# **OpenAI Poaches top Google deal-maker!**
OpenAI has lured away Albert Lee, the Google executive who spearheaded corporate development for Google Cloud and DeepMind--including the massive $32 billion Wiz acquisition bid--**[to lead its own strategic investments and mergers](https://substack.com/redirect/cc9a6695-4605-4e12-b6ab-0bf897fdcc65?j=eyJ1IjoiMWh2cjYwIn0.VBabVq-VHATXCJqsddI7uMpdiTm0WkVanRDZfvCWMkg)** as the AI race intensifies.
| [](https://substack.com/redirect/68c2f875-6078-4b5c-9202-71d06ffa151e?j=eyJ1IjoiMWh2cjYwIn0.VBabVq-VHATXCJqsddI7uMpdiTm0WkVanRDZfvCWMkg)|
---|---|---
* **Lee 's Proven Track Record**: Lee oversaw several high-profile Google acquisitions, bringing deep M&A expertise to OpenAI's growing leadership team.
* **OpenAI 's Acquisition Spree**: They recently acquired Neptune for AI training tools, Statsig for $1.1 billion, io for over $6 billion, and Software Applications Incorporated.
* **Building Executive Bench** : Lee joins recent hires like former Slack CEO Denise Dresser as chief revenue officer and ex-Instacart CEO Fidji Simo as head of applications.
* **Valuation Surge Milestone** : OpenAI's valuation has skyrocketed to $500 billion since launching ChatGPT in late 2022, fueling its aggressive growth strategy.

Its lawyers represent industry players throughout the entire business cycle, from the formation of a corporation or licensing relationship, through marketing, partnering, growth, and disputes, to profitable exits. We're proud to share that all partners at Ifrah Law have been ranked by Chambers and Partners, the gold standard for evaluating legal excellence worldwide. | | ---|---|--- AI infrastructure stocks plungeover financing worries The AI infrastructure with shares of Broadcom tumbling 5.6%, Oracle sliding 2.7%, and CoreWeave dropping about 8% amid mounting investor worries over financing massive data center expansions. | | ---|---|--- Broadcom Faces Margin Pressure : Despite forecasting AI chip sales doubling to $8.2 billion, lower gross margins on AI systems contributed to an 11% drop last week and further declines. Oracle 's Debt Burden Soars: Capital expenditures jumped to $50 billion with lease commitments up 148% to $248 billion, fueling concerns over its 500% debt-to-equity ratio versus peers. CoreWeave Hits Debt Risks : The Nvidia GPU cloud provider, down over 60% from its June high, carries a 120% debt-to-equity ratio, amplifying selloff pressures. Investors Demand Proven ROI : Market skepticism is growing on funding sustainability, even as AI demand surges and experts note potential revenue from more compute capacity. Pentagon grabs stake in massive Tennessee smelter The Pentagon is acquiring in Tennessee through a partnership with South Korea's Korea Zinc, aiming to produce 540,000 tons annually and reduce U.S. reliance on China-dominated supply chains. | | ---|---|--- Share Sale Details : Korea Zinc will issue $1.9 billion in new shares to a U.S. government-controlled joint venture, giving it and strategic investors 10% ownership in the company. Priority Access Granted : The agreement ensures the United States gains priority access to Korea Zinc's expanded global critical minerals production starting in 2026. National Security Boost : Officials emphasize the deal strengthens defense, semiconductors, AI, and advanced manufacturing by rebuilding domestic processing absent since the 1970s. Broader Trump Strategy : This follows similar Pentagon investments, including a major stake in rare earth miner MP Materials and an $8.5 billion framework with Australia. Google's real estate move could spell doom for Zillow Zillow shares out about $1.6 billion in market value, after Google began testing real estate listings directly in its search results--sparking fears of heightened long-term competition in the online property portal space. | | ---|---|--- Google Tests Listings : Google is experimenting with for-sale property listings in select markets on mobile, powered by HouseCanary, enabling users to view details, request tours, and contact agents without leaving search. Limited Near-Term Impact : Analysts expect minimal immediate effects, as most of Zillow's traffic (228 million users in Q3) comes directly via its app and site rather than organic search. Potential Long-Term Risks : A nationwide rollout could gradually erode portal traffic, raise acquisition costs, and pressure lead generation and monetization over years. Analyst Views Mixed : Goldman Sachs sees Google's move as a long-term headwind; Wells Fargo anticipates higher traffic expenses but no disintermediation; Oppenheimer warns of multi-year implications if expanded. Oil prices plunge to 7-year low Crude oil futures with U.S. benchmark WTI dropping 2.13% to $55.61 per barrel and Brent falling 1.93% to $59.39, amid surging OPEC+ supply and fading Ukraine war risks. | | ---|---|--- OPEC+ Boosts Production Rapidly : After years of output cuts, the group has ramped up supply, creating a market surplus that's driving prices lower this year. Geopolitical Tensions Ease Significantly : Prospects of a Ukraine-Russia peace deal under Trump administration pressure has reduced threats of supply disruptions that have plagued the market since since the 2022 invasion. Worst Annual Drop Looming : WTI down 22% year-to-date (steepest since 2018), while Brent has fallen nearly 20% (worst since 2020). U.S. Gasoline Prices Collapse : Average pump prices dipped below $3 per gallon nationwide, hitting the lowest level in four years, per AAA data. OpenAI Poaches top Google deal-maker! OpenAI has lured away Albert Lee, the Google executive who spearheaded corporate development for Google Cloud and DeepMind--including the massive $32 billion Wiz acquisition bid-- as the AI race intensifies. | | ---|---|--- Lee 's Proven Track Record: Lee oversaw several high-profile Google acquisitions, bringing deep M&A expertise to OpenAI's growing leadership team. OpenAI 's Acquisition Spree: They recently acquired Neptune for AI training tools, Statsig for $1.1 billion, io for over $6 billion, and Software Applications Incorporated. Building Executive Bench : Lee joins recent hires like former Slack CEO Denise Dresser as chief revenue officer and ex-Instacart CEO Fidji Simo as head of applications. Valuation Surge Milestone : OpenAI's valuation has skyrocketed to $500 billion since launching ChatGPT in late 2022, fueling its aggressive growth strategy. The Capitalist is a reader-supported publication Reject Corporate Left Wing Journalism You're currently a free subscriber to . For the full experience, | | | | | | | (C) 2025 Matthew Miller 548 Market Street PMB 72296, San Francisco, CA 94104 ? ?

Senator Gillibrand

| | Dorian Gray glanced at the picture, and suddenly an uncontrollable feeling of hatred for Basil Hallward came over him, as though it had been suggested to him by the image on the canvas, whispered into his ear by those grinning lips. The mad passions of a hunted animal stirred within him, and he loathed the man who was seated at the table, more than in his whole life he had ever loathed anything. He glanced wildly around. Something glimmered on the top of the painted chest that faced him. His eye fell on it. He knew what it was. It was a knife that he had brought up, some days before, to cut a piece of cord, and had forgotten to take away with him. He moved slowly towards it, passing Hallward as he did so. As soon as he got behind him, he seized it and turned round. Hallward stirred in his chair as if he was going to rise. He rushed at him and dug the knife into the great vein that is behind the ear, crushing the man’s head down on the table and stabbing again and again. There was a stifled groan and the horrible sound of some one choking with blood. Three times the outstretched arms shot up convulsively, waving grotesque, stiff-fingered hands in the air. He stabbed him twice more, but the man did not move. Something began to trickle on the floor. He waited for a moment, still pressing the head down. Then he threw the knife on the table, and listened. He could hear nothing, but the drip, drip on the threadbare carpet. He opened the door and went out on the landing. The house was absolutely quiet. No one was about. For a few seconds he stood bending over the balustrade and peering down into the black seething well of darkness. Then he took out the key and returned to the room, locking himself in as he did so. The thing was still seated in the chair, straining over the table with bowed head, and humped back, and long fantastic arms. Had it not been for the red jagged tear in the neck and the clotted black pool that was slowly widening on the table, one would have said that the man was simply asleep. How quickly it had all been done! He felt strangely calm, and walking over to the window, opened it and stepped out on the balcony. The wind had blown the fog away, and the sky was like a monstrous peacock’s tail, starred with myriads of golden eyes. He looked down and saw the policeman going his rounds and flashing the long beam of his lantern on the doors of the silent houses. The crimson spot of a prowling hansom gleamed at the corner and then vanished. A woman in a fluttering shawl was creeping slowly by the railings, staggering as she went. Now and then she stopped and peered back. Once, she began to sing in a hoarse voice. The policeman strolled over and said something to her. She stumbled away, laughing. A bitter blast swept across the square. The gas-lamps flickered and became blue, and the leafless trees shook their black iron branches to and fro. He shivered and went back, closing the window behind him.
---
Dorian Gray glanced at the picture, and suddenly an uncontrollable feeling of hatred for Basil Hallward came over him, as though it had been suggested to him by the image on the canvas, whispered into his ear by those grinning lips. The mad passions of a hunted animal stirred within him, and he loathed the man who was seated at the table, more than in his whole life he had ever loathed anything. He glanced wildly around. Something glimmered on the top of the painted chest that faced him. His eye fell on it. He knew what it was. It was a knife that he had brought up, some days before, to cut a piece of cord, and had forgotten to take away with him. He moved slowly towards it, passing Hallward as he did so. As soon as he got behind him, he seized it and turned round. Hallward stirred in his chair as if he was going to rise. He rushed at him and dug the knife into the great vein that is behind the ear, crushing the man’s head down on the table and stabbing again and again. There was a stifled groan and the horrible sound of some one choking with blood. Three times the outstretched arms shot up convulsively, waving grotesque, stiff-fingered hands in the air. He stabbed him twice more, but the man did not move. Something began to trickle on the floor. He waited for a moment, still pressing the head down. Then he threw the knife on the table, and listened. He could hear nothing, but the drip, drip on the threadbare carpet. He opened the door and went out on the landing. The house was absolutely quiet. No one was about. For a few seconds he stood bending over the balustrade and peering down into the black seething well of darkness. Then he took out the key and returned to the room, locking himself in as he did so. The thing was still seated in the chair, straining over the table with bowed head, and humped back, and long fantastic arms. Had it not been for the red jagged tear in the neck and the clotted black pool that was slowly widening on the table, one would have said that the man was simply asleep. How quickly it had all been done! He felt strangely calm, and walking over to the window, opened it and stepped out on the balcony. The wind had blown the fog away, and the sky was like a monstrous peacock’s tail, starred with myriads of golden eyes. He looked down and saw the policeman going his rounds and flashing the long beam of his lantern on the doors of the silent houses. The crimson spot of a prowling hansom gleamed at the corner and then vanished. A woman in a fluttering shawl was creeping slowly by the railings, staggering as she went. Now and then she stopped and peered back. Once, she began to sing in a hoarse voice. The policeman strolled over and said something to her. She stumbled away, laughing. A bitter blast swept across the square. The gas-lamps flickered and became blue, and the leafless trees shook their black iron branches to and fro. He shivered and went back, closing the window behind him.
---

A Humanoid Robot You Have to See to Believe

At CES, Nvidia’s CEO dropped a bombshell:
“This is the ChatGPT moment for robotics.”
What followed stunned even seasoned analysts.A humanoid robot—powered by advanced AI—demonstrated real-world tasks with shocking precision.
