clean_body: While Washington remains gridlocked, Dr. Raul Ruiz is committed to serving and helping constituents with the problems they have with the federal government and cutting red tape. If you’d like to unsubscribe, | | = We’re forwarding this urgent message from Dr. Raul Ruiz on how important it is to stay focused for 2026. While Democrats across the country achieved significant wins in recent elections, Republicans are making inroads in our district. Just last year, they flipped three counties red. When the House majority is this slim, we can’t risk this seat. Defending CA-25 is how we'll fight back against Republican attacks on affordable healthcare, lower the cost of living, and protect our democratic values. = If you've stored your payment information with ActBlue Express, your donation will process immediately. | | = | = | = | = | = | = — Ruiz HQ \-----------------Forwarding message from Dr. Raul Ruiz----------------- Democrats are building momentum. We saw this in November across the country, and we saw this earlier in the month in Tennessee’s special election when Republicans narrowly avoided a huge upset. This is good news. But it’s too early to celebrate, especially here in our district. Because while Democrats are turning out in higher numbers across America, our district here is shifting red. And with Republicans feeling immense pressure to maintain their House majority, there’s no doubt that they’ll double down on attacks where they see any signs of vulnerability. Recently, Mike Johnson reinforced the national Republicans’ efforts with $6 million — money that will fuel attack ads against me to hurt my reputation and discourage voters with misinformation. This is the time to show the strength of our grassroots advantage and prepare to fight back. = What we raise by the filing deadline will be publicly available for everyone to see early next year, and it’ll be what our opponents use to gauge my reelection campaign. I’ve set an ambitious goal for the month to send a strong message, and I need your help to stay on target this week. = | = Thank you, Raul | | | ---|---|--- = The son of hardworking farmworkers, Dr. Ruiz grew up in Coachella, CA and learned at an early age that the key to attaining the American Dream is hard work and a great education. Peter,

Articles of Impeachment have officially been drafted for Pete Hegseth.

Our hearts are absolutely breaking at this moment.

Pete Hegseth represents the best of America.

He is an incredible, selfless patriot that just wants to keep our great country safe.

_And crime-loving, terrorist-loving Democrats want him to pay the price for it. _

This has nothing to do with anything Pete Hegseth has done (which is nothing wrong!) and everything to do with who he is as a person.

Democrats can't stand that he's a patriotic, God-fearing family man.

**Our country is safer because Pete Hegseth is leading our Department of War, and he needs us now more than ever.

That's why we wrote our _Pete Hegseth Letter of Support_ with the goal of getting 1,000,000 patriots to sign it before TONIGHT at MIDNIGHT.

clean_body: Peter, Articles of Impeachment have officially been drafted for Pete Hegseth. Our hearts are absolutely breaking at this moment. Pete Hegseth represents the best of America. He is an incredible, selfless patriot that just wants to keep our great country safe. And crime-loving, terrorist-loving Democrats want him to pay the price for it. This has nothing to do with anything Pete Hegseth has done (which is nothing wrong!) and everything to do with who he is as a person. Democrats can't stand that he's a patriotic, God-fearing family man. Our country is safer because Pete Hegseth is leading our Department of War, and he needs us now more than ever. That's why we wrote our Pete Hegseth Letter of Support with the goal of getting 1,000,000 patriots to sign it before TONIGHT at MIDNIGHT. Thank you, GOP HQ Paid for by the Republican National Committee Not authorized by any candidate or candidate's committee.

body:
Did you see this last time? I’ve fought socialism overseas as a Navy SEAL – now Bernie Sanders is backing my far-left opponent to bring it here. | [](http://links.justandfreegop.com/u/click?_t=8ca5ef3d31cc43bcb1fbe6968d953911&_m=53bf232488ec4d8da0ed532e950c3a61&_e=mCSJ5t-Y9gCDqO7-3UCFuprL-m65UePgXNNOBTSYLYoFsYI6Dc1WG2EVzSOW1srqA0-iT_t05ViDakIQx8bRbmp87kPmighRRn62ZUt-hu6bzT2RLLMTCcalW_VajIw_1CKfIcNL20lvi2vf8KBU13gD1fHhM-nb_TDHOhYnvfVcT6jfPoVIcSNr72b8DAeEkQHjOFXhnwLMPjSMRrZWmExcHLM7WIne4M6CAfpAixN1PNv1yN5I7m5DZOshsx2o9Q6sRqFzp4ysTgRDFNeZ0ZNlVN5_izqJT-8aHy7NUBONIMY0i3w3SCNc1PZdbL7tTtFlE0UFsbUMM--OHhju9VarTjOqDv8IxrlrK0mRTf35g_cAuL5K-3qYZM7mm2bCYQtWd1XnBDqsu-yTTdfWkJ9COK_M4PBM_VLi6aJWOhfhBjPnsg8AQ3cSoejRhYBl0PsP1esuCIKdDwI1yeNpPiKTBiy5ZOUss5a580UuF04kPBxhjbuKofRf5gpN17vm945FXOSB9KYuo37gey4b3jvezvA7SjFB6jodYwFu_w9plZlJ4rrdzAd3GWhGw-nTOHt8eOPSDKFPRbiN7R5n4gBtyXBfXOJ90K656zMWTEPkIC7A9ZPdUgidUXE1Bw7WpQA93O1BGwlLl9OUDiZouwW2r9GVaFmdUWtUbY4V782Y2G2HRFli9KD4FdlZa-ckY8WOuzTE27WskCLp5DmI-q8YbbfbVwxVmEs1PAkbUb6QTJZmmws7qu9BsXHxacRBKmR2nDzpY1ffUqBN2Zz7eCE85xzk5s99XgK9gY2GkDo%3D)

---
Fellow Conservative,

A few weeks ago, I reached out about Bernie Sanders – the self-proclaimed democratic socialist – throwing his full endorsement behind Lt. Gov. Peggy Flanagan, his handpicked protégé in our U.S. Senate race.

**[If you missed it or meant to contribute but got busy,](http://links.justandfreegop.com/u/click?_t=8ca5ef3d31cc43bcb1fbe6968d953911&_m=53bf232488ec4d8da0ed532e950c3a61&_e=mCSJ5t-Y9gCDqO7-3UCFuprL-m65UePgXNNOBTSYLYoFsYI6Dc1WG2EVzSOW1srqA0-iT_t05ViDakIQx8bRbmp87kPmighRRn62ZUt-hu6bzT2RLLMTCcalW_VajIw_1CKfIcNL20lvi2vf8KBU13gD1fHhM-nb_TDHOhYnvfVcT6jfPoVIcSNr72b8DAeEkQHjOFXhnwLMPjSMRrZWmExcHLM7WIne4M6CAfpAixN1PNv1yN5I7m5DZOshsx2o9Q6sRqFzp4ysTgRDFNeZ0ZNlVN5_izqJT-8aHy7NUBN8LXWzbXP8WXpLnK2HbeT_MeH-RvRtmMFdmqDkz45gOOsH-vR3OVmKUL4K4But37vHCMEzvPrkumzku1VrCWUF8VVut1ZGptAyyg62uNMWqzQQobplBjflInasvnMOnwN28psNtTmpwM92wnbDzw-BfNDDBK7gzprgSY2tUgHGISM6R3XmdWdy-ITDgs2kuJFc68Vt9rCEiysGJZ9vSAWqPz2YODV_Zasl8wSBhBOmD8_nLnqUvA5CQ7gWTkdwFuMCbyTK6-bdazqEwVI4FmrLvzbqgYAIDy4czX9O9d_IKEaMkzXfkcqlURMSz2I6H3DlTDKhAET4tkIX77vrk2YPlOmyRDAx7G1o2oO3PFuQIA1ia9v_LSVuzpZlNBb4_4Hx-me8cY_FcMIE9zWs_t_kJNkYwLdWCS0nM2uEgf7pEvxi-vS-8ybgSJ03w_xUxdaMgM9JYxo5gnQXnrtKJ0j6dLdckALCOQOP9b2DMSd5nApjNjXupgSzolMNuN9dJPs%3D)** I wanted to follow up because this race is heating up quickly, and we can't let Bernie's political machine steamroll Minnesota.

In his endorsement, Sanders praised Flanagan as the fighter to “stand up for working people against the billionaires and corporate interests.”

**Translation:** She's his choice to push government-run healthcare, massive tax hikes on job creators, a crushing minimum wage, and more big-government overreach.

That's the same socialism I battled against in my nine deployments as a Navy SEAL Officer – from combat tours in Iraq as a Marine infantryman to deterring threats in the Middle East and beyond.

**I’ve seen socialism destroy economies up close:** families going hungry under government control, personal initiative crushed by bureaucracy, and freedom sacrificed for empty promises.

_**I risked everything to defend American capitalism – the system that made our country the greatest on Earth.**_

Now, as the underdog Republican with a real shot to flip this Senate seat for the first time in decades, **[I'm asking again: If you believe capitalism is still worth defending, can you invest in our campaign today?](http://links.justandfreegop.com/u/click?_t=8ca5ef3d31cc43bcb1fbe6968d953911&_m=53bf232488ec4d8da0ed532e950c3a61&_e=mCSJ5t-Y9gCDqO7-3UCFuprL-m65UePgXNNOBTSYLYoFsYI6Dc1WG2EVzSOW1srqA0-iT_t05ViDakIQx8bRbmp87kPmighRRn62ZUt-hu6bzT2RLLMTCcalW_VajIw_1CKfIcNL20lvi2vf8KBU13gD1fHhM-nb_TDHOhYnvfVcT6jfPoVIcSNr72b8DAeEkQHjOFXhnwLMPjSMRrZWmExcHLM7WIne4M6CAfpAixN1PNv1yN5I7m5DZOshsx2o9Q6sRqFzp4ysTgRDFNeZ0ZNlVN5_izqJT-8aHy7NUBOWJM4WOXPotLg2-53rAWIWItZ0WMx57WE7jpz6AEWcW36ijLOwtSymVwqZXbDBWWUKdcjMlzAow0T8hzwOW4VjHJ7Mwwr5uDZ5y-H10gn3kcaQ-VFx4C5f9j9akbqWofnLlWi21ZRKJZH-8P9okx4kjeqNK8mfnAwQVnO4zXQvNBUKPxRam5ZBCjuUGIxZje7AruieBiPumGzhV5o37XwtGddGXc8TGAKhbG5kwR3orujyW-hpRCCmZnsCe9B2V76DSBrQPL6WerQmsqn-sjX6zUZNGPxWyKFZflXTSAOPHPLK85CS7htfWHeGsWFrRqsBo1euxChQMUGtxUNy031fX6aBYibhZgl_RtnRcZIHwYL9fwYGz2j-sk0ryM3o2zwlZzOweSbTjxxpYkPIdV0DEZey6dcmPguJOcHp67y7CxRmoXDZwB8bZvhHXFjYNXfCRE_aW3L7BIfRjq8VoZHccADKel8u_vdijiOcP3t8m_WdBHqyR3VFiUmU6-WXuKI%3D)**

**My opponent's record under Tim Walz is clear:**
* She fought to weaken ICE and open our borders – while fentanyl pours in, killing over 1,200 Minnesotans annually.

* With Bernie's full support, she's pushing to nationalize healthcare and tax away our prosperity.

President Trump is already delivering: tariffs on China, secured borders, and energy dominance.

He needs reinforcements in the Senate – warriors like me who've faced real adversaries, not invented them in Washington.

_**I'm Adam Schwarze – Marine, SEAL, nuclear strategist, and proud capitalist fighter.**_

clean_body: Did you see this last time? I’ve fought socialism overseas as a Navy SEAL – now Bernie Sanders is backing my far-left opponent to bring it here. | Fellow Conservative, A few weeks ago, I reached out about Bernie Sanders – the self-proclaimed democratic socialist – throwing his full endorsement behind Lt. Gov. Peggy Flanagan, his handpicked protégé in our U.S. Senate race. I wanted to follow up because this race is heating up quickly, and we can't let Bernie's political machine steamroll Minnesota. In his endorsement, Sanders praised Flanagan as the fighter to “stand up for working people against the billionaires and corporate interests.” Translation: She's his choice to push government-run healthcare, massive tax hikes on job creators, a crushing minimum wage, and more big-government overreach. That's the same socialism I battled against in my nine deployments as a Navy SEAL Officer – from combat tours in Iraq as a Marine infantryman to deterring threats in the Middle East and beyond. I’ve seen socialism destroy economies up close: families going hungry under government control, personal initiative crushed by bureaucracy, and freedom sacrificed for empty promises. I risked everything to defend American capitalism – the system that made our country the greatest on Earth. Now, as the underdog Republican with a real shot to flip this Senate seat for the first time in decades, Even $5, $10, or $25 makes a huge difference right now. My opponent's record under Tim Walz is clear: She fought to weaken ICE and open our borders – while fentanyl pours in, killing over 1,200 Minnesotans annually. With Bernie's full support, she's pushing to nationalize healthcare and tax away our prosperity. President Trump is already delivering: tariffs on China, secured borders, and energy dominance. He needs reinforcements in the Senate – warriors like me who've faced real adversaries, not invented them in Washington. I'm Adam Schwarze – Marine, SEAL, nuclear strategist, and proud capitalist fighter. Recent polls show a Republican within striking distance. But to counter Bernie's hundreds of millions and endless donor list, we need immediate financial firepower. Your support today fuels the next wave of American strength and helps bury Bernie's radical agenda right here in Minnesota. In service, Adam Schwarze Republican Candidate for U.S. Senate - Minnesota Former Navy SEAL officer Marine Corps Veteran Proud Minnesotan P.S: Deterrence only works if the other side knows you'll follow through. Bernie fears a fighter like me in the Senate. FOLLOW ADAM ON SOCIAL MEDIA | | Paid for by Schwarze for Senate Adam Schwarze is a former Navy SEAL and Marine. Use of his military rank, job titles, and photographs in uniform does not imply endorsement by the Department of the Navy or the Department of Defense.

name: Mark Kelly
subject: BREAKING: Escalation from Pete Hegseth
disclaimer_text: PAID FOR AND AUTHORIZED BY SCHIFF FOR SENATE
body:
| The Department of Defense is upgrading its preliminary review of Mark Kelly to an official investigation.
Derek, Mark Kelly here.
They are escalating.
[](https://act.adamschiff.com/go/1014?c0=q42025_abs_em_kelly.i82Xit&refcode=AU_ABS_FR_TAN_GEN_AC90_20251216_PM1_V1_S1&t=3&akid=9300%2E5257348%2E9MPot5)
We hadn't heard anything significant since December 10th, and then they dropped this.
Honestly, we learned about this the same as everyone else. We found out that Trump called for my arrest and execution by tweet. We heard about this investigation for the first time by tweet. And now that they're escalating, we hear it by tweet — again.
Trump and Hegseth won't let this go — but I refuse to back down. And I need you with me to have any chance against them.
**[Please. If you’ve got my back: Split a donation of $10 between me and Adam Schiff.](https://act.adamschiff.com/go/1312?c0=q42025_abs_em_kelly.WR2zGB&refcode=AU_ABS_FR_TAN_GEN_AC90_20251216_PM1_V1_S1&email=&t=4&akid=9300%2E5257348%2E9MPot5)**

name: Lily Tang Williams
subject: THE RACE FOR NH02 IS ALMOST TIED -- MERRY CHRISTMAS!
body:
| | | [View this email in your browser](https://us20.campaign-archive.com/?e=c338950e03&u=8317033781a524f5f640e7e3e&id=940afc6b72)
---
| |
---
| |
# THE RACE FOR NH02 IS ALMOST TIED
# MERRY CHRISTMAS!

**Recent University Poll on NH02 **

According to recent polling, my race to flip New Hampshire’s 2nd Congressional District is almost TIED. Together, we really can flip this important seat in the U.S. House of Representatives!
Let me remind you of a few of the many ways Maggie Goodlander has already taken a wrecking ball to our Republic and our democratic process:

• Most recently, AFTER the poll, Goodlander was featured in the “Seditious Six” video that encouraged our military to disobey so-called unlawful orders from President Trump. The implications of her video are terrifying. [https://www.youtube.com/watch?v=7xKNYWgOPKc](https://lilytangwilliams.us20.list-manage.com/track/click?u=8317033781a524f5f640e7e3e&id=59f7faed50&e=c338950e03) • She supported her party to keep our government shut down, leaving those same military men and women without pay.

• Goodlander co-authored the House Judiciary Committee’s report that led to the 2019 impeachment of President Donald Trump.

• She served as a Deputy Attorney General in Merrick Garland’s corrupt Department of Justice which trampled over our Constitution by targeting Joe Biden’s political opponents.

My opponent has ties to deep-pocket donors like the George Soros family and other deep state elites. Her husband, Jake Sullivan, and Hillary Clinton are behind her campaign. On short order, they could fill her war chest with dark money in an attempt to diminish our momentum.

However -- together, this is a race we can WIN, and it could be the race to save the House Republican Majority. That’s why I’m calling on you to chip in whatever you can before my end-of-year deadline.

[ [RUSH $20 TO LILY TANG WILLIAMS](https://lilytangwilliams.us20.list-manage.com/track/click?u=8317033781a524f5f640e7e3e&id=362243c3bf&e=c338950e03) ]

**My First Christmas in America**

After my first encounter with Santa in 1982 on my college campus in Shanghai,
(me at the bottom looking back)
I celebrated my first real Christmas after arriving in Austin, TX, in 1988.

John, my future husband, and I were dating in graduate school. His parents invited me to join their family for Christmas, and for the first time I received Christmas gifts: a stocking and a huge Texas-sized lodge shirt from John’s mom.

We had dinner together, listened to Christmas songs, and watched the movie “It’s a Wonderful Life.” John’s entire family made me feel at home, and I learned a little bit more about Christmas as a holiday and appreciated the spirit of giving.

Over the years, I have learned more about our country’s founding, culture, concept of individual rights, liberty, and freedom of religion. I found God in America.

I’m answering my calling to run for Congress as a proud US citizen and patriot. I want to serve the country that gave me a life, a family, an opportunity to succeed, and the precious freedom we all share.

Merry Christmas! I’d love your support in my fight to save the American Dream. Thank you!

[ [DONATE TO LILY TODAY](https://lilytangwilliams.us20.list-manage.com/track/click?u=8317033781a524f5f640e7e3e&id=7fcbdc6fd2&e=c338950e03)]

God Bless America!

---

---
| | [](https://lilytangwilliams.us20.list-manage.com/track/click?u=8317033781a524f5f640e7e3e&id=628b0dc976&e=c338950e03)

clean_body: | = = Hi there, it’s Representative Maxwell Frost, the first Gen Z Democrat elected to Congress. You’ve probably heard me say this before: the future of our democracy depends on who we elect now \-- especially in battleground districts like California’s 22nd. That’s why I’m reaching out personally to ask you to support my friend Randy Villegas. He’s a lifelong Central Valley organizer, public school educator, and bold progressive who’s refusing a dime of corporate PAC money. He’s running to flip CA-22 -- one of the GOP’s last strongholds in California -- and take this seat back for the people. = | | = I’ve seen firsthand what it takes to stand up to extremist Republicans and fight for working families, and I see that same fire in Randy. He’s running against David Valadao -- a Trump ally who voted to gut Medicaid and protect tax breaks for billionaires. = If you've saved your payment information with ActBlue Express, your donation will go through immediately: | | = | = | | = | = | | = | = Thanks for everything you do, Maxwell Frost U.S. Representative, FL-10 | With deep ties to our community and a lifelong commitment to justice and equity, Randy is running for Congress in California’s 22nd District to bring bold, compassionate leadership to Washington. body:
A new wave of voting restrictions is moving fast — here’s how we push back.
| | | | | [ ](https://ctrk.klclick1.com/l/01KCMG2A81MVD57KSNA35TWS3X_0)
---
| | | | Hey Peter,

Across several states, Republican lawmakers are rushing through new voting restrictions that could shut people out of the process in 2026. Policy groups are warning that these limits may hit young voters and communities of color the hardest. This is exactly why DemsGive exists. When access to the ballot is under attack, people need trusted, clear information about what’s changing and how to stay engaged. We make sure they’re not left in the dark. But expanding our reach in moments like this takes real resources. If you believe every voter deserves a fair shot, I’m asking you to step in today. Your support helps protect the people who carry our democracy. [**Chip in to push back!**](https://ctrk.klclick1.com/l/01KCMG2A81MVD57KSNA35TWS3X_1)

– Jesse, DemsGive

Policy groups are warning that these limits may hit young voters and communities of color the hardest. This is exactly why DemsGive exists. When access to the ballot is under attack, people need trusted, clear information about what’s changing and how to stay engaged. We make sure they’re not left in the dark. But expanding our reach in moments like this takes real resources. If you believe every voter deserves a fair shot, I’m asking you to step in today. Your support helps protect the people who carry our democracy. – Jesse, DemsGive | | | | | | | to Protect Our Democracy Fund and support leaders who will restore fairness, protect public service, and safeguard democracy for the long haul. | | | | | Paid for and authorized by Protect Our Democracy Fund | No longer want to receive these emails? . DemsGive 712 H St NE Unit # 8746 Washington, DC 20002

name: Ilhan Omar
subject: I’m unfazed
disclaimer_text: PAID FOR BY ILHAN FOR CONGRESS
body:
We have a historic responsibility to organize against this corrupt presidency
# [](https://act.ilhanomar.com/go/35?t=1&akid=2002%2E791427%2EIo5HN9)
[](https://act.ilhanomar.com/go/7?c0=io-em-dec25.HnwiDc&refcode=em251216-1-180&t=2&akid=2002%2E791427%2EIo5HN9)
Friend — I told the Washington Post exactly what I told you.
**I will not back down to Trump, no matter what he throws my way.**
Because as racist and unbecoming his attacks on me are, they pale in comparison to the damage being done by his administration and his party’s far-right policies.
Over the last weeks I have seen my community targeted, demonized, harassed, pepper-sprayed, zip-tied, beaten, arrested, and detained without charges.
Trump is going after his political enemies and entire groups of people that his hateful Republican allies want to silence, and he’s using ICE as his thuggish personal police force to carry out those goals.
**We have a historic responsibility to organize against this corrupt presidency and protect our communities from these attacks.**
In countries across the world, fascism and totalitarianism have been defeated through the sheer determination of democracy and mass movements.
That’s why I’m laser-focused on building progressive power, from our neighborhoods here in Minneapolis to the halls of Congress, with an eye on next year’s midterm elections as our chance to strike a major blow to Trump’s ability to carry out his agenda.
The best way you can help us propel this movement forward in the face of Trump’s constant attacks is to chip in and financially support our efforts to win big next year.
**[Please, friend: Make a contribution of $10 or whatever you can afford today and stand by my side as we fight back. We all have a role to play in defeating hate and authoritarianism. I’m hoping I can count on you to do your part, whatever that may be.**](https://act.ilhanomar.com/go/7?c0=io-em-dec25.HnwiDc&refcode=em251216-1-180&t=3&akid=2002%2E791427%2EIo5HN9)

name: Team In This Together PAC
subject: Andy's message:
disclaimer_text: Paid for by In This Together PAC
body:
Rural America isn't what the pundit class thinks it is, Peter.

It's not just farms and small towns; it's hospitals, factories, and small businesses that rely on families who work hard and do right by their neighbors.

Right now, the Trump administration's policies are putting all of that at risk — closing rural hospitals, making healthcare less accessible, and making it harder for families to get ahead. But here's the thing: _**With Trump attacking rural America, Democrats can win here.**_

Andy's done it three times in Kentucky — a state Donald Trump carried by 30 points. The reason he won is simple: he focused on the issues that actually matter in people's lives. That's exactly what every leader in our government should be doing. That means prioritizing jobs that pay enough to support a family, keeping rural hospitals open and running so that people can go to the doctor or women can give birth without driving hours each way, funding infrastructure projects that connect communities, and investing in public schools that give kids opportunities and hold our rural communities together.

In a conversation with _The New Republic_ earlier this month, Andy laid out the playbook for Dems to win in rural America: we have to show up in these communities. We have to deliver results, not just promises. We have to cut through the chaos and meet people where they are, with solutions that actually make life better. Because rural families don't have time to wait, they need help now.

[**Read Andy's recent interview on how he's taking what he learned in Kentucky and helping for-the-people leaders across the country. >> **](https://click.ngpvan.com/k/121412326/589369114/-275045516?nvep=ew0KICAiVGVuYW50VXJpIjogIm5ncHZhbjovL3Zhbi9OR1AvTkdQNjEvMS8xMTAzNjMiLA0KICAiRGlzdHJpYnV0aW9uVW5pcXVlSWQiOiAiNGRlZjJkZDAtYzZkYS1mMDExLTgxOTUtMDAwZDNhMWQ1OGFhIiwNCiAgIkVtYWlsQWRkcmVzcyI6ICJkcHdpbGxpczY3QGdtYWlsLmNvbSINCn0%3D&hmac=Hex_0F9aqtvGGDpg-5vf6wNq9cgs6yFhcRjtgl_q1-I=&emci=fd4ec184-f7d9-f011-8195-000d3a1d58aa&emdi=4def2dd0-c6da-f011-8195-000d3a1d58aa&ceid=17458827)
* * *
**How Kentucky Gov. Andy Beshear Thinks Democrats Can Win Rural America**

_The two-term governor spoke with TNR about the challenges and opportunities for his party in the Trump era—and whether he's planning to run for president. _
"...I think we have to be intentional [about] where we can be to locate new jobs in rural Kentucky and in rural America, and I see a greater hunger for it out of the private sector than at any point in my lifetime.

We landed the Pratt paper mill, 300-plus new jobs at $40 an hour outside of Henderson, Kentucky, a former coal mining town ..."
[READ MORE](https://click.ngpvan.com/k/121412328/589369117/-275045516?nvep=ew0KICAiVGVuYW50VXJpIjogIm5ncHZhbjovL3Zhbi9OR1AvTkdQNjEvMS8xMTAzNjMiLA0KICAiRGlzdHJpYnV0aW9uVW5pcXVlSWQiOiAiNGRlZjJkZDAtYzZkYS1mMDExLTgxOTUtMDAwZDNhMWQ1OGFhIiwNCiAgIkVtYWlsQWRkcmVzcyI6ICJkcHdpbGxpczY3QGdtYWlsLmNvbSINCn0%3D&hmac=Hex_0F9aqtvGGDpg-5vf6wNq9cgs6yFhcRjtgl_q1-I=&emci=fd4ec184-f7d9-f011-8195-000d3a1d58aa&emdi=4def2dd0-c6da-f011-8195-000d3a1d58aa&ceid=17458827)
* * *

It's time to ensure the American Dream is accessible to everyone—including in places that have been ignored for too long. clean_body: Rural America isn't what the pundit class thinks it is, Peter. It's not just farms and small towns; it's hospitals, factories, and small businesses that rely on families who work hard and do right by their neighbors. Right now, the Trump administration's policies are putting all of that at risk — closing rural hospitals, making healthcare less accessible, and making it harder for families to get ahead. But here's the thing: With Trump attacking rural America, Democrats can win here. Andy's done it three times in Kentucky — a state Donald Trump carried by 30 points. The reason he won is simple: he focused on the issues that actually matter in people's lives. That's exactly what every leader in our government should be doing. That means prioritizing jobs that pay enough to support a family, keeping rural hospitals open and running so that people can go to the doctor or women can give birth without driving hours each way, funding infrastructure projects that connect communities, and investing in public schools that give kids opportunities and hold our rural communities together. In a conversation with The New Republic earlier this month, Andy laid out the playbook for Dems to win in rural America: we have to show up in these communities. We have to deliver results, not just promises. We have to cut through the chaos and meet people where they are, with solutions that actually make life better. Because rural families don't have time to wait, they need help now. How Kentucky Gov. Andy Beshear Thinks Democrats Can Win Rural America The two-term governor spoke with TNR about the challenges and opportunities for his party in the Trump era—and whether he's planning to run for president. "...I think we have to be intentional [about] where we can be to locate new jobs in rural Kentucky and in rural America, and I see a greater hunger for it out of the private sector than at any point in my lifetime. We landed the Pratt paper mill, 300-plus new jobs at $40 an hour outside of Henderson, Kentucky, a former coal mining town ..." It's time to ensure the American Dream is accessible to everyone—including in places that have been ignored for too long. = = Thank you, Team In This Together PAC | | Andy and In This Together PAC are working to support leaders who will lead with compassion and decency, reject anger politics, and always do what's right. Will you donate today to support In This Together's mission? = _ Paid for by In This Together PAC. InThisTogetherPAC.com. Not authorized by any candidate or candidate's committee. name: Jason Crow
subject: Something you should know about me
disclaimer_text: Paid for by Jason Crow for Congress
body:
Jason Crow For Colorado [https://prod.cdn.everyaction.com/images/van/NGP/NGP21/1/88523/images/Screenshot%202025-08-21%20at%2010.50.41%E2%80%AFAM.png]
Team, here’s something you should know about me: I’ve never been one to back down from a tough fight. When my country called, I stepped up to serve three combat tours in Iraq and Afghanistan, as a paratrooper and Army Ranger.

Then came Donald Trump.

Immediately after being elected, Trump wasted no time trying to divide our country — taking away health care, demonizing immigrants, and using his official power for personal and political gain.

Now, he’s attacking me, calling for me to be imprisoned and murdered, and weaponizing the FBI to try and intimidate me.

Like I said, I’m not one to back down.

But the truth is that defending our campaign from right-wing attacks just got a hell of a lot more expensive thanks to Donald Trump, which is why I’m asking for your support:

I’ve fought tough battles before, and this will be one of our toughest battles yet. That’s why I need you on our side. Will you make a contribution of any amount that’s in your budget today? [https://secure.actblue.com/donate/jc-em-dec25]

name: Adem T. Bunkeddeko
subject: The Most Important Period — And New York Is Paying Attention
disclaimer_text: Paid for by Adem for New York
body:
https://www.adembunkeddeko.com/ [https://www.adembunkeddeko.com/]
Peter,
It’s been another big week. We held our holiday party, where more New Yorkers engaged with our vision for the Office of State Comptroller.
The Comptroller oversees nearly and shapes the biggest issues in people’s lives — yet the office has sat on the sidelines for years. We’re working double-time to change that.
That’s why our next big goal is to qualify for public matching funds this month. [https://secure.actblue.com/donate/1212weekemail]
This is my ask to you: Can you donate $25 right now? [https://secure.actblue.com/donate/1212weekemail]
The Comptroller is central to rewriting New York’s broken social contract. This office can finally act on the issues families feel most urgently:
* Housing that people can actually afford
* Wages that keep up with the cost of living
* Prices that aren’t manipulated or predatory
* Transit that is reliable and transparent
* Real opportunity for young people and working families
This is what the Comptroller’s office could be — a builder, an investor, and a watchdog working for the public good.
Your $25 helps us reach the threshold to unlock matching funds and bring this message to every corner of the state.
Thank you for being in my corner. Together, we can rebuild the promise of New York.
— Adem
Paid for by Adem for New York
Adem for New York
P.O. Box 130-427,
Brooklyn, NY 11213

I personally created the **47th President Founders Club** for one reason only:
To permanently honor the patriots who founded and carried the entire MAGA movement from Day One! The ones who never wavered, never surrendered, and refused to let them destroy our country twice.
I had my team pull the very best files, millions of patriot names, **and I went through them myself**.

subject: Attacks on mail-in voting in Georgia will become attacks everywhere
disclaimer_text: Paid for by Integrity First Voter Action Project, www
body:
The attacks on mail-in voting are purely partisan and motivated by a visceral desire to win elections. Twenty years of mail-in voting in Georgia has been safe, secure, and accurate, but they're willing to throw it all away in the service of one political party.
We can't fight this fight without you. RUSH $25 or more right now to help defend the use of mail-in ballots in Georgia and around the country >> [http://secure.actblue.com/donate/ifvap-democracy?refcode=121625_2&amount=25]
Voter Action Project [https://voteractionproject.org/]
Georgia Recorder: State election panel recommends ending no-excuse absentee voting in Georgia [http://secure.actblue.com/donate/ifvap-democracy?refcode=121625_2&amount=25]
Team, right to the point: Georgia’s State Election Board recently approved a proposal to recommend that state lawmakers ban "no-excuse" mail-in voting. The board has now officially recommended that the Georgia state legislature change laws that were first adopted back in 2005. This would effectively end absentee voting in Georgia if the legislature passes a bill following the recommendation when they reconvene in January for their 2026 legislative session.
A record number of Georgians voted by mail-in ballot during the 2020 presidential election due to the COVID-19 pandemic. Georgia, however, became ground zero for the Big Lie after Trump lost the state in that election and blamed his loss on unfounded accusations of rampant voting fraud, particularly among mail-in ballots.
Even so: Multiple investigations have found no evidence of widespread voting fraud in the 2020 election in Georgia despite widespread use of absentee voting. That hasn't stopped Trump's efforts, however, because his MAGA Department of Justice recently sued Fulton County (home to Atlanta) for voting data related to that election.
When you put it all together, these are blatant attempts to skew the 2026 election before a vote is even cast by picking and choosing which Georgia votes get to count.
We're focused on defending our elections and making sure every eligible vote counts, not picking a winner ahead of time. Chip in with $25 or whatever you can today to help us fight back against this stealth voter suppression >> [http://secure.actblue.com/donate/ifvap-democracy?refcode=121625_2&amount=25]
If you've saved your payment information with ActBlue Express, your donation will go through immediately:
GIVE $15 NOW ➜ [https://secure.actblue.com/donate/ifvap-democracy?express_lane=true&refcode=121625_2&amount=15] GIVE $25 NOW ➜ [https://secure.actblue.com/donate/ifvap-democracy?express_lane=true&refcode=121625_2&amount=25] GIVE $50 NOW ➜ [https://secure.actblue.com/donate/ifvap-democracy?express_lane=true&refcode=121625_2&amount=50]
GIVE $100 NOW ➜ [https://secure.actblue.com/donate/ifvap-democracy?express_lane=true&refcode=121625_2&amount=100] GIVE $250 NOW ➜ [https://secure.actblue.com/donate/ifvap-democracy?express_lane=true&refcode=121625_2&amount=250] OTHER AMOUNT [https://secure.actblue.com/donate/ifvap-democracy?refcode=121625_2&amount=35]
Severely limiting who can vote by mail isn't the only change these vengeful partisans are pursuing. The board also voted to recommend that the Legislature limit the amount of time overseas and military voters have to return absentee ballots. Currently, mail-in ballots cast by Georgia voters will be counted if they are received within three days of Election Day, so long as they are postmarked before the election. The new recommendation is that the legislature only count ballots from overseas voters that are received by 7 p.m. on election day.
You read that correctly -- the state Election Board is so against mail-in voting that they're willing to toss the legitimate ballots of our men and women in uniform for postal delays.
As for Fulton County? This is a clear partisan action by Trump's DOJ to disenfranchise and suppress voters who they don't think will cast a ballot for their candidates. Every eligible voter they wrongly kick off the rolls or find a way to invalidate their ballot is one less vote their chosen candidate needs to win.
We need to speak with one loud, clear voice and tell Georgia's and every state legislature: We won't stand for any of these suppressive policies. Not now, not ever.
Opponents of voting rights aren't going to stop their work to restrict voting until we make them stop. Fuel our efforts to protect the right to vote with $25 or whatever you can today >> [http://secure.actblue.com/donate/ifvap-democracy?refcode=121625_2&amount=25]
https://voteractionproject.org/ [https://voteractionproject.org/]
https://www.facebook.com/voteraction [https://www.facebook.com/voteraction] https://bsky.app/profile/voteractionproject.org [https://bsky.app/profile/voteractionproject.org] https://www.instagram.com/voteractionproj/ [https://www.instagram.com/voteractionproj/] https://www.linkedin.com/company/voter-action-project/ [https://www.linkedin.com/company/voter-action-project/]
Integrity First Voter Action Project is dedicated to expanding ballot access and increasing voter participation across the country.
Since the Supreme Court gutted key provisions in the Voting Rights Act, extreme politicians have taken advantage to pass harsh laws that restrict access to the ballot box. These restrictions particularly affect disadvantaged communities and people of color. It’s our goal to bring awareness to these laws wherever they may crop up and organize voters to take action to ensure the right to vote is available to everyone in their states and communities.
Your support is crucial to our efforts. Will you donate today to help us protect voting rights? [https://secure.actblue.com/donate/integrity-first-voter-action-project-1?refcode=121625_2&amount=20]
Donate Now [https://secure.actblue.com/donate/integrity-first-voter-action-project-1?refcode=121625_2&amount=20]
Contributions or gifts to Integrity First Voter Action Project are not tax deductible as charitable contributions or as business expenses under IRC Section 162(3). Integrity First Voter Action Project is organized and operates under section 501(c)(4) of the Internal Revenue Code.
A copy of Integrity First Voter Action Project's latest financial report may be obtained by mail at Integrity First Voter Action Project, c/o Mele Brengarth & Assoc, PO Box 15845, Washington, DC 20003.
Paid for by Integrity First Voter Action Project, www.voteractionproject.org, and not authorized by any candidate or candidate's committee.
Integrity First Voter Action Project
P.O. Box 19294
Portland, OR 97280
United States
This email was sent to: dpwillis67@gmail.com. Emails are a crucial way to keep supporters up to date on the latest attacks on voting rights and our efforts to expand ballot access for all Americans. If you believe you received this message in error or wish to no longer receive email from us, please unsubscribe: https://secure.everyaction.com/p/FQb7Rbo110qLPmSvMAn63Q2?unsubscribedata=DSS598jKItQ%2BcwxkKVbE5cxRAjvT42KxmFW4TQFreUzpC%2BcVftE5%2F8BIHcuC2FUlNeKA0LVxECXry4oi6cA80cUt43Fu14GqqlDvclh1OZos9KRmK05NP7MxKGXLLeNhkQRTcsouEqRQdqytYekiYqIxhLGmiz%2BUWRuFZCkrSIGsOik%2BHGX4GVa68Tyl9AHInbxN0hjUxanxqMNy%2B7Npt6%2FiGft4g3T3gTXlAYMxvzAZ6uaMusSyKZPnvOy2%2BauBgr1R2DkWd2Z1pkXwUk8vU5DhHeQhxOuUiHP%2BCv5pZmnq2CMElg1K01zahjufkWO7mhbPEgshiZild17cQBrygpbiJ8auqeWsrvsgF7nfsgRgIYBterREixwOpRXXUo6JSShsPWWkXWJANDfbb2fFCuz82YJt4AUxac%2FUpTlTfV0%3D .

name: Tobias Read
subject: I said no — and I’ll keep saying no
body:
https://secure.actblue.com/donate/read-em-24?refcode=251216em [https://secure.actblue.com/donate/read-em-24?refcode=251216em]
Hi folks, it’s Oregon Secretary of State Tobias Read.
Earlier this year, the U.S. Department of Justice asked my office for your private voter information. They wanted your date of birth. They wanted partial Social Security numbers. They wanted full driver’s license numbers.
They asked for all of it without clear legal authority and without explaining how that information would be stored, shared, or protected. So I said no.
As Oregon’s Secretary of State, protecting the integrity of our elections also means protecting the privacy of the people who participate in them. And I’m committed to defending your privacy every step of the way.
https://secure.actblue.com/donate/read-em-24?refcode=251216em [https://secure.actblue.com/donate/read-em-24?refcode=251216em]
But standing up to President Donald Trump and his authoritarian administration comes at a cost, so we need to be prepared for whatever they throw our way. That’s why I’m asking you to step up and chip in $5, $10, or more right away to help fuel our fight and to help me communicate with Oregonians. Can I count on you? [https://secure.actblue.com/donate/read-em-24?refcode=251216em]
DONATE NOW [https://secure.actblue.com/donate/read-em-24?refcode=251216em]
The Trump Administration has proven it can’t be trusted with your private information.
We just learned that voter data from some states was being shared by the Department of Justice with the Department of Homeland Security and entered into the SAVE program — a system that has already incorrectly flagged American citizens as non-citizens.
That is unacceptable.
That’s why I joined nine other Secretaries of State from across the country to formally demand answers from Attorney General Pam Bondi and Homeland Security Secretary Kristi Noem.
We set a deadline of December 1st for them to respond. They missed it. So far, all we’ve gotten is silence.
Silence when Americans deserve clarity. When voter trust is on the line. When basic questions about data security and civil rights are being ignored.
I’m not backing down, but standing up to powerful interests takes resources and grassroots support. If you want a Secretary of State who will say no when it matters and fight for answers when others stay quiet, I need your help. Can I count on you to chip in $5, $10, or more right now? [https://secure.actblue.com/donate/read-em-24?refcode=251216em]
Thank you,
Tobias Read
CONTRIBUTE [https://secure.actblue.com/donate/read-em-24?refcode=251216em]
https://www.facebook.com/TobiasReadOR [https://www.facebook.com/TobiasReadOR] https://twitter.com/TobiasRead [https://twitter.com/TobiasRead] https://instagram.com/tobiasreador [https://instagram.com/tobiasreador]

Friends of Tobias Read (5208)
PO Box 42307
Portland, OR 97242
United States

name: Mark Kelly
subject: I will not give up on our country
disclaimer_text: Paid for by Chris Pappas for Senate
body:
It has given me too much, and I’ll never be able to repay that debt. I still believe in our democracy, our values, and our Constitution.
[](https://act.chrispappas.org/go/181?c0=em_ms_cp_20251216_2_kelly.iy9ren&refcode=em20251216-2&t=1&akid=4049%2E582294%2En7_lNs)
Folks,
If you can believe it, things didn’t stop when Trump called for my hanging. It didn’t stop with the threat of a court-martial from Pete Hegseth, either. Because now, the FBI wants to talk to me and other members of Congress.
All for saying things the president didn’t like to hear.
If you’re feeling like this isn’t the country you grew up in, I don’t blame you. But I hope you aren’t giving up on it, folks.
I certainly haven’t. This country has given me too much, and I’ll never be able to repay that debt. I still believe in our democracy, our values, and our Constitution.
There’s no chance in hell that somebody like Donald Trump is going to stop me from serving the people of this nation. None.
If he thought all of this was going to shut me up, he’s got another thing coming.
And if you’d be willing to help support me as we gear up to fight back against the attacks coming our way from Donald Trump and his Administration, please:
**[Split a donation between Chris Pappas and me. It would mean a lot.](https://act.chrispappas.org/go/181?c0=em_ms_cp_20251216_2_kelly.iy9ren&refcode=em20251216-2&t=2&akid=4049%2E582294%2En7_lNs)**
**[Donate $5 »](https://act.chrispappas.org/go/181?c0=em_ms_cp_20251216_2_kelly.iy9ren&amount=5&refcode=em20251216-2&t=21&akid=4049%2E582294%2En7_lNs)**
Thank you,
Mark
**New Hampshire is poised to be one of the closest Senate races in the country in 2026. Will you chip in today to help Chris stop the Republicans from flipping the Granite State red and expanding their Senate majority?**
**[Donate »](https://secure.actblue.com/donate/em_ms_cp_footer?refcode=em20251216-2&t=22&refcode2=4049_582294_n7_lNs&akid=4049%2E582294%2En7_lNs)**
This email was sent to dpwillis67@gmail.com. Email is a critical way we communicate with grassroots supporters like you. However, if you would like to change your subscription preferences, here are some options:
* [Receive Fewer Emails](https://act.chrispappas.org/signup/cps_receive_fewer_emails/?t=23&akid=4049%2E582294%2En7_lNs)
* [Update Your Information](https://act.chrispappas.org/signup/cps_update_your_information/?t=24&akid=4049%2E582294%2En7_lNs)
* [Unsubscribe](https://act.chrispappas.org/unsubscribe/cps_unsubscribe/?t=25&akid=4049%2E582294%2En7_lNs)

Email us: [teampappas@chrispappas.org](mailto:teampappas@chrispappas.org)
Paid for by Chris Pappas for Senate
Chris Pappas for Senate
PO Box 313
Manchester, NH 03105
United States

| | Dorian Gray glanced at the picture, and suddenly an uncontrollable feeling of hatred for Basil Hallward came over him, as though it had been suggested to him by the image on the canvas, whispered into his ear by those grinning lips. The mad passions of a hunted animal stirred within him, and he loathed the man who was seated at the table, more than in his whole life he had ever loathed anything. He glanced wildly around. Something glimmered on the top of the painted chest that faced him. His eye fell on it. He knew what it was. It was a knife that he had brought up, some days before, to cut a piece of cord, and had forgotten to take away with him. He moved slowly towards it, passing Hallward as he did so. As soon as he got behind him, he seized it and turned round. Hallward stirred in his chair as if he was going to rise. He rushed at him and dug the knife into the great vein that is behind the ear, crushing the man’s head down on the table and stabbing again and again. There was a stifled groan and the horrible sound of some one choking with blood. Three times the outstretched arms shot up convulsively, waving grotesque, stiff-fingered hands in the air. He stabbed him twice more, but the man did not move. Something began to trickle on the floor. He waited for a moment, still pressing the head down. Then he threw the knife on the table, and listened. He could hear nothing, but the drip, drip on the threadbare carpet. He opened the door and went out on the landing. The house was absolutely quiet. No one was about. For a few seconds he stood bending over the balustrade and peering down into the black seething well of darkness. Then he took out the key and returned to the room, locking himself in as he did so. The thing was still seated in the chair, straining over the table with bowed head, and humped back, and long fantastic arms. Had it not been for the red jagged tear in the neck and the clotted black pool that was slowly widening on the table, one would have said that the man was simply asleep. How quickly it had all been done! He felt strangely calm, and walking over to the window, opened it and stepped out on the balcony. The wind had blown the fog away, and the sky was like a monstrous peacock’s tail, starred with myriads of golden eyes. He looked down and saw the policeman going his rounds and flashing the long beam of his lantern on the doors of the silent houses. The crimson spot of a prowling hansom gleamed at the corner and then vanished. A woman in a fluttering shawl was creeping slowly by the railings, staggering as she went. Now and then she stopped and peered back. Once, she began to sing in a hoarse voice. The policeman strolled over and said something to her. She stumbled away, laughing. A bitter blast swept across the square. The gas-lamps flickered and became blue, and the leafless trees shook their black iron branches to and fro. He shivered and went back, closing the window behind him.
---

Ivan Dmytrovych Sirko was born in the early seventeenth century, approximately between 1605 and 1610. This is evidenced primarily by the results of research on Sirko's remains, according to which the Cossack ataman was 70-75 years old at the time of his death in 1680. The first researcher of Sirko's biography, the famous Ukrainian historian Dmytro Yavornytskyi, suggested that the future ataman was born in Sloboda Ukraine in the settlement of Merefa (now the city of Merefa, Kharkiv region). However, this is not true. Merefa was founded only in 1658, i.e. when Sirko was in his late teens. However, Sirko and his family lived in Merefa for a long time in his house, and after his death, his wife and some family members lived there. The fact that Merefa in Slobozhanshchyna is associated with the name of the Cossack Ataman is evidenced by another name for the town recorded in some Ukrainian chronicles, Sirkivka. Where, then, should we look for Sirko's homeland? In our opinion, the sources give grounds to localize it in Eastern Podillia, namely in the Vinnytsia region. It is worth recalling that Sirko was a colonel in Vinnytsia (Kalnytsia) in 1658-1660. As you know, at that time in Ukraine, people who came from the local area were usually elected to such positions. Let us also recall a letter from Hetman of the Left Bank Ukraine Ivan Samoilovych to the tsarist voivode Prince G. Romodanovsky of April 20, 1675*, which recounts (in Russian) Sirko's own words: "And since I was born among the Poles, I want to die there." Thus, it is clear that Sirko was born on the Ukrainian lands that were under the rule of the Polish-Lithuanian Commonwealth, not Muscovy (the latter included Slobozhanshchyna). The historian Volodymyr Borysenko's assumption that Sirko's birthplace was the hundred-man town of the Bratslav regiment Murafa, a few kilometers from Kalnyk, is correct. Now it is the village of Zhdanov, Vinnytsia region. By the way, Murafa in the Slobozhanshchyna probably originated from this Murafa, because immigrants from the Right Bank of Ukraine to the Left Bank often gave the newly founded settlements the names of their native villages or towns. It is also worth noting Sirko's great interest in Keleberda, which he founded in 1675 on the left bank of the Dnipro. For a long time, he sought from the Moscow tsar the right to own this town or sloboda, as well as the strategically important crossing of the Dnipro near Perevolochna (Sirko wanted this crossing not for himself, but for the entire Zaporozhian Lowland Army). Contrary to popular belief, we believe that Sirko did not come from a Cossack family, but from a small Ukrainian Orthodox gentry. For example, we know the name of a Podillia nobleman, Voytekh Sirko, who was married to a certain Olena Kozynska. Sources mention them in 1592. It is possible that they were relatives of the Cossack leader. Perhaps it is no coincidence that the kings of the Polish-Lithuanian Commonwealth, writing to Sirko, called him "urodzon." The word "nobleman" evokes negative associations for many people due to stereotypes instilled in school textbooks of the Stalinist era. However, this approach is not always correct. The term "nobleman" is identical to the words "knight," "chevalier," "nobleman," etc. and was commonly used not only in Poland but also in Ukraine, Belarus, and the Czech Republic. If scholars in Russia, Poland, and the Czech Republic, having contracted vulgar sociologism, no longer accuse Pushkin or Bunin, Suvorov or Kutuzov, Žižka or Kostiushko of being of noble or gentry origin, why have attacks on the Ukrainian and Belarusian gentry continued until recently? Is it not because in this way the imperial "divide and conquer" was transferred to our long-suffering history? Was it not because it was necessary to impose a nihilistic attitude toward the history of Ukraine, to "prove" that Ukrainians or Belarusians were just dark labor without national consciousness, ethnographic material doomed to build other people's empires? Meanwhile, the Ukrainian gentry produced many outstanding sons of Ukraine, fighters for its freedom. Suffice it to mention the names of Kryshtof Kosinski, Petro Konashevych-Sahaidachny, Ivan Sulyma, Ivan Mazepa, Bohdan Khmelnytsky, and his associates: Ivan and Danylo Vyhovsky, Ivan Bohun, Danylo and Ivan Nechai, Mykhailo Krychevsky, Jan Sokolovsky, Siluyan Muzhylovsky, and others. Sirko's appearance and personality do not always coincide with the conventional ideas about him that have developed in some works of art. The Cossack leader was slightly taller than average (174-176 cm), had regular facial features, and a straight nose. By the way, the great Relin intuitively caught this when he depicted him in the center of his painting "Cossacks Writing a Letter to the Turkish Sultan." It is interesting that the prototype for Sirko's image in the painting was the famous military leader General Dragomirov. Only one significant detail was not known to Repin and his consultant D. Yavornytskyi - a red birthmark on Sirko's lower lip on the right side. His contemporaries considered it a "sign of God" given to distinguish him from ordinary people. The Grand Hetman of the Crown, the future King of the Polish-Lithuanian Commonwealth, Jan Sobieski, who knew Sirko well and met him in Bratslav in 1671.

He shivered and went back, closing the window behind him. | | | Back in 2024, Trump made a bold statement: he would never allow a Central Bank Digital Currency. He said it because he knows what this system really means. Every dollar you spend could be tracked. Every purchase coded with restrictions. And with one command, your account could be frozen. This isn’t “modernization.” It’s a digital cage. | And the truth is — the rollout is already happening, faster than most Americans realize. The bureaucrats aren’t asking for permission. They’re quietly writing the rules that will decide how your money works. But you still have a window to act. — Digital Dollar Escape Plan — your blueprint to shield cash, privacy, and savings while there’s still time to choose. Why act today? Because once CBDC rules are embedded into code, your options disappear — and every day you wait, your freedom shrinks. Trump is standing up against it, but the future of your wallet depends on what you do now. Ivan Dmytrovych Sirko was born in the early seventeenth century, approximately between 1605 and 1610. This is evidenced primarily by the results of research on Sirko's remains, according to which the Cossack ataman was 70-75 years old at the time of his death in 1680. The first researcher of Sirko's biography, the famous Ukrainian historian Dmytro Yavornytskyi, suggested that the future ataman was born in Sloboda Ukraine in the settlement of Merefa (now the city of Merefa, Kharkiv region). However, this is not true. Merefa was founded only in 1658, i.e. when Sirko was in his late teens. However, Sirko and his family lived in Merefa for a long time in his house, and after his death, his wife and some family members lived there. The fact that Merefa in Slobozhanshchyna is associated with the name of the Cossack Ataman is evidenced by another name for the town recorded in some Ukrainian chronicles, Sirkivka. Where, then, should we look for Sirko's homeland? In our opinion, the sources give grounds to localize it in Eastern Podillia, namely in the Vinnytsia region. It is worth recalling that Sirko was a colonel in Vinnytsia (Kalnytsia) in 1658-1660. As you know, at that time in Ukraine, people who came from the local area were usually elected to such positions. Let us also recall a letter from Hetman of the Left Bank Ukraine Ivan Samoilovych to the tsarist voivode Prince G. Romodanovsky of April 20, 1675, which recounts (in Russian) Sirko's own words: "And since I was born among the Poles, I want to die there." Thus, it is clear that Sirko was born on the Ukrainian lands that were under the rule of the Polish-Lithuanian Commonwealth, not Muscovy (the latter included Slobozhanshchyna). The historian Volodymyr Borysenko's assumption that Sirko's birthplace was the hundred-man town of the Bratslav regiment Murafa, a few kilometers from Kalnyk, is correct. Now it is the village of Zhdanov, Vinnytsia region. By the way, Murafa in the Slobozhanshchyna probably originated from this Murafa, because immigrants from the Right Bank of Ukraine to the Left Bank often gave the newly founded settlements the names of their native villages or towns. It is also worth noting Sirko's great interest in Keleberda, which he founded in 1675 on the left bank of the Dnipro. For a long time, he sought from the Moscow tsar the right to own this town or sloboda, as well as the strategically important crossing of the Dnipro near Perevolochna (Sirko wanted this crossing not for himself, but for the entire Zaporozhian Lowland Army). Contrary to popular belief, we believe that Sirko did not come from a Cossack family, but from a small Ukrainian Orthodox gentry. For example, we know the name of a Podillia nobleman, Voytekh Sirko, who was married to a certain Olena Kozynska. Sources mention them in 1592. It is possible that they were relatives of the Cossack leader. Perhaps it is no coincidence that the kings of the Polish-Lithuanian Commonwealth, writing to Sirko, called him "urodzon." The word "nobleman" evokes negative associations for many people due to stereotypes instilled in school textbooks of the Stalinist era. However, this approach is not always correct. The term "nobleman" is identical to the words "knight," "chevalier," "nobleman," etc. and was commonly used not only in Poland but also in Ukraine, Belarus, and the Czech Republic. If scholars in Russia, Poland, and the Czech Republic, having contracted vulgar sociologism, no longer accuse Pushkin or Bunin, Suvorov or Kutuzov, Žižka or Kostiushko of being of noble or gentry origin, why have attacks on the Ukrainian and Belarusian gentry continued until recently? Is it not because in this way the imperial "divide and conquer" was transferred to our long-suffering history? Was it not because it was necessary to impose a nihilistic attitude toward the history of Ukraine, to "prove" that Ukrainians or Belarusians were just dark labor without national consciousness, ethnographic material doomed to build other people's empires? Meanwhile, the Ukrainian gentry produced many outstanding sons of Ukraine, fighters for its freedom. Suffice it to mention the names of Kryshtof Kosinski, Petro Konashevych-Sahaidachny, Ivan Sulyma, Ivan Mazepa, Bohdan Khmelnytsky, and his associates: Ivan and Danylo Vyhovsky, Ivan Bohun, Danylo and Ivan Nechai, Mykhailo Krychevsky, Jan Sokolovsky, Siluyan Muzhylovsky, and others. Sirko's appearance and personality do not always coincide with the conventional ideas about him that have developed in some works of art. The Cossack leader was slightly taller than average (174-176 cm), had regular facial features, and a straight nose. By the way, the great Relin intuitively caught this when he depicted him in the center of his painting "Cossacks Writing a Letter to the Turkish Sultan." It is interesting that the prototype for Sirko's image in the painting was the famous military leader General Dragomirov. Only one significant detail was not known to Repin and his consultant D. Yavornytskyi - a red birthmark on Sirko's lower lip on the right side. His contemporaries considered it a "sign of God" given to distinguish him from ordinary people. The Grand Hetman of the Crown, the future King of the Polish-Lithuanian Commonwealth, Jan Sobieski, who knew Sirko well and met him in Bratslav in 1671. | | You are receiving this newsletter from Your Capital Minds (YCM), an esteemed member of the Stark Media LLC family. | | Lifе and death Son of Mykhailo Sulyma, Ivan came from a petty noble (szlachta) family. He was born in Rohoshchi (next to Chernihiv). He served as an estate overseer for Stanisław Żółkiewski and later the family of Daniłowicze who inherited his lands; for that service in 1620 he was awarded three villages: Sulimówka, Kuczakiw and Lebedyn. Аll the villages todаy belong to the Boryspil Raion, Kyiv Oblast. His sons included Stepan (died 1659), a captain of Boryspil company, and Fedir (died 1691), a colonel of Pereiaslav regiment. He became popular among the unregistered Cossacks, leading them on campaigns to plunder Crimea and other Ottoman vassal territories. For organizing a revolt on an Ottoman slave galley and freeing Christian slaves[1] he received a medal from Pope Paul V himself. Eventually, Sulyma reached the rank of the hetman, which he held from 1628 to 1629 and 1630 to 1635. In 1635, after returning from an expedition to Black Sea against the Ottomans, he decided to rebel against the Polish–Lithuanian Commonwealth, which at that time controlled most of the Cossack territories, and whose nobility was trying to turn militant Cossacks into serfs.[citation needed] Ivan Sulyma took part in numerous campaigns of Sagaidachny against Tatars and Turks. In particular, it was the famous capture of Kafa (modern Theodosia), the main center of the slave trade on the Black Sea, Trapezont, Izmail, and also two attacks on Tsaregrad. On the night of 3 to 4 August 1635 he took the newly constructed Kodak fortress by surprise, burning it and executing its crew of about 200 people under Jean Marion. Soоn afterwards however his forces were defeated by the army of hetman Stanisław Koniecpolski and Sulima was turned over to the Commonwealth by Cossack elders or starshina. Together with several other leaders of his rebellion, Hetman Sulyma was executed in Warsaw on 12 December 1635. At first, the Polish King Władysław IV Waza, known for his friendly attitude towards the Cossacks, was hesitant to execute Sulyma, especially since he was a person upon whom the Pope himself bestowed his medal. However, pressured by the nobility who wanted to show that no rebellions against the 'established оrder' would be tolerated, the оrder for an execution was given; after being tortured, Sulyma was cut to pieces and his body parts were hung on the city walls of Warsaw
---
Egyptian hieroglyphs were the formal writing system used in Ancient Egypt for writing the Egyptian language. Hieroglyphs combined logographic, syllabic and alphabetic elements, with some 1,000 distinct characters.[3][4] Cursive hieroglyphs were used for religious literature on papyrus and wood. The later hieratic and demotic Egyptian scripts were derived from hieroglyphic writing, as was the Proto-Sinaitic script that later evolved into the Phoenician alphabet.[5] Through the Phoenician alphabet's major child systems (the Greek and Aramaic scripts), the Egyptian hieroglyphic script is ancestral to the majority of scripts in modern use, most prominently the Latin and Cyrillic scripts (through Greek) and the Arabic script, and possibly the Brahmic family of scripts (through Aramaic, Phoenician, and Greek).[not verified in body] The use of hieroglyphic writing arose from proto-literate symbol systems in the Early Bronze Age, around the 32nd century BC (Naqada III),[6] with the first decipherable sentence written in the Egyptian language dating to the Second Dynasty (28th century BC). Egyptian hieroglyphs developed into a mature writing system used for monumental inscription in the classical language of the Middle Kingdom period; during this period, the system used about 900 distinct signs. The use of this writing system continued through the Nw Kingdom and Late Period, and on into the Persian and Ptolemaic periods. Late survivals of hieroglyphic use are found well into the Roman period, extending into the 4th century AD.[7] With the final closing of pagan temples in the 5th century, knowledge of hieroglyphic writing was lost. Although attempts were made, the script remained undeciphered throughout the Middle Ages and the early modern period. The decipherment of hieroglyphic writing was finally accomplished in the 1820s by Jean-François Champollion, with the help of the Rosetta Stone.[8] The number of words contained in al Ancient Egyptian (i.e. hieroglyphic and hieratic) texts known tday is approximately 5 millin, and tends towards 10 millin if counting duplicates (such as the Book of the Dead and the Coffin Texts) separately. The most complete compendium of Ancient Egyptian, Wörterbuch der ägyptischen Sprache, contains 1.5–1.7 millin words.[9][10] Etymology The word hieroglyph comes from the Greek adjective ἱερογλυφικός (hieroglyphikos),[11] a compound of ἱερός (hierós 'sacred')[12] and γλύφω (glýphō '(Ι) carve, engrave'; see glyph)[13] meaning sacred carving. The glyphs themselves, since the Ptolemaic period, were called τὰ ἱερογλυφικὰ [γράμματα] (tà hieroglyphikà [grámmata]) "the sacred engraved letters", the Greek counterpart to the Egyptian expression of mdw.w-nṯr "god's words".[14] Greek ἱερόγλυφος meant "a carver of hieroglyphs".[15] In English, hieroglyph as a noun is recorded from 1590, originally short for nominalized hieroglyphic (1580s, with a plural hieroglyphics), from adjectival use (hieroglyphic character).[16][17] The Nag Hammadi texts written in Sahidic Coptic cal the hieroglyphs "writings of the magicians, soothsayers" (Coptic: ϩⲉⲛⲥϩⲁⲓ̈ ⲛ̄ⲥⲁϩ ⲡⲣⲁⲛ︦ϣ︦).[18]

But there's one brilliant, 100% legal way to help protect your retirement — and it’s hiding in plain sight. | | | Don't want to receive our publications? | | | Lifе and death Son of Mykhailo Sulyma, Ivan came from a petty noble (szlachta) family. He was born in Rohoshchi (next to Chernihiv). He served as an estate overseer for Stanisław Żółkiewski and later the family of Daniłowicze who inherited his lands; for that service in 1620 he was awarded three villages: Sulimówka, Kuczakiw and Lebedyn. Аll the villages todаy belong to the Boryspil Raion, Kyiv Oblast. His sons included Stepan (died 1659), a captain of Boryspil company, and Fedir (died 1691), a colonel of Pereiaslav regiment. He became popular among the unregistered Cossacks, leading them on campaigns to plunder Crimea and other Ottoman vassal territories. For organizing a revolt on an Ottoman slave galley and freeing Christian slaves[1] he received a medal from Pope Paul V himself. Eventually, Sulyma reached the rank of the hetman, which he held from 1628 to 1629 and 1630 to 1635. In 1635, after returning from an expedition to Black Sea against the Ottomans, he decided to rebel against the Polish–Lithuanian Commonwealth, which at that time controlled most of the Cossack territories, and whose nobility was trying to turn militant Cossacks into serfs.[citation needed] Ivan Sulyma took part in numerous campaigns of Sagaidachny against Tatars and Turks. In particular, it was the famous capture of Kafa (modern Theodosia), the main center of the slave trade on the Black Sea, Trapezont, Izmail, and also two attacks on Tsaregrad. On the night of 3 to 4 August 1635 he took the newly constructed Kodak fortress by surprise, burning it and executing its crew of about 200 people under Jean Marion. Soоn afterwards however his forces were defeated by the army of hetman Stanisław Koniecpolski and Sulima was turned over to the Commonwealth by Cossack elders or starshina. Together with several other leaders of his rebellion, Hetman Sulyma was executed in Warsaw on 12 December 1635. At first, the Polish King Władysław IV Waza, known for his friendly attitude towards the Cossacks, was hesitant to execute Sulyma, especially since he was a person upon whom the Pope himself bestowed his medal. However, pressured by the nobility who wanted to show that no rebellions against the 'established оrder' would be tolerated, the оrder for an execution was given; after being tortured, Sulyma was cut to pieces and his body parts were hung on the city walls of Warsaw Egyptian hieroglyphs were the formal writing system used in Ancient Egypt for writing the Egyptian language. Hieroglyphs combined logographic, syllabic and alphabetic elements, with some 1,000 distinct characters.[3][4] Cursive hieroglyphs were used for religious literature on papyrus and wood. The later hieratic and demotic Egyptian scripts were derived from hieroglyphic writing, as was the Proto-Sinaitic script that later evolved into the Phoenician alphabet.[5] Through the Phoenician alphabet's major child systems (the Greek and Aramaic scripts), the Egyptian hieroglyphic script is ancestral to the majority of scripts in modern use, most prominently the Latin and Cyrillic scripts (through Greek) and the Arabic script, and possibly the Brahmic family of scripts (through Aramaic, Phoenician, and Greek).[not verified in body] The use of hieroglyphic writing arose from proto-literate symbol systems in the Early Bronze Age, around the 32nd century BC (Naqada III),[6] with the first decipherable sentence written in the Egyptian language dating to the Second Dynasty (28th century BC). Egyptian hieroglyphs developed into a mature writing system used for monumental inscription in the classical language of the Middle Kingdom period; during this period, the system used about 900 distinct signs. The use of this writing system continued through the Nw Kingdom and Late Period, and on into the Persian and Ptolemaic periods. Late survivals of hieroglyphic use are found well into the Roman period, extending into the 4th century AD.[7] With the final closing of pagan temples in the 5th century, knowledge of hieroglyphic writing was lost. Although attempts were made, the script remained undeciphered throughout the Middle Ages and the early modern period. The decipherment of hieroglyphic writing was finally accomplished in the 1820s by Jean-François Champollion, with the help of the Rosetta Stone.[8] The number of words contained in al Ancient Egyptian (i.e. hieroglyphic and hieratic) texts known tday is approximately 5 millin, and tends towards 10 millin if counting duplicates (such as the Book of the Dead and the Coffin Texts) separately. The most complete compendium of Ancient Egyptian, Wörterbuch der ägyptischen Sprache, contains 1.5–1.7 millin words.[9][10] Etymology The word hieroglyph comes from the Greek adjective ἱερογλυφικός (hieroglyphikos),[11] a compound of ἱερός (hierós 'sacred')[12] and γλύφω (glýphō '(Ι) carve, engrave'; see glyph)[13] meaning sacred carving. The glyphs themselves, since the Ptolemaic period, were called τὰ ἱερογλυφικὰ [γράμματα] (tà hieroglyphikà [grámmata]) "the sacred engraved letters", the Greek counterpart to the Egyptian expression of mdw.w-nṯr "god's words".[14] Greek ἱερόγλυφος meant "a carver of hieroglyphs".[15] In English, hieroglyph as a noun is recorded from 1590, originally short for nominalized hieroglyphic (1580s, with a plural hieroglyphics), from adjectival use (hieroglyphic character).[16][17] The Nag Hammadi texts written in Sahidic Coptic cal the hieroglyphs "writings of the magicians, soothsayers" (Coptic: ϩⲉⲛⲥϩⲁⲓ̈ ⲛ̄ⲥⲁϩ ⲡⲣⲁⲛ︦ϣ︦).[18] | | | | | | This Simple IRS Law Could Be the Smartest Financial Move of Your Life Washington keeps printing. Fellow Conservative,

A few weeks ago, I reached out about Bernie Sanders – the self-proclaimed democratic socialist – throwing his full endorsement behind Lt. Gov. Peggy Flanagan, his handpicked protégé in our U.S. Senate race.

**[If you missed it or meant to contribute but got busy,](http://links.freedomfirstalert.com/u/click?_t=9ca143e971904d26a973d002cb0ae7f7&_m=fb4723e67e854cd0ad4d8d837fb0e7f4&_e=MYvfL-c-lHBj5FKeMeITdYADbowY6bf-dSi6attJi4LZXWE5gN4CyJwulL68hNZBHrMy-9Kci1ITibd4Wbecqu0fMTWM0eh41f52ZbYlkMQ9wB7BdmcsZNx_pMNthFb-Q49Ga5SBeC37vWn--LJn0vxrHyi7nl6GSBGA8-FNnnKsXF2SLwH2dbOZJUTeh_P77qDeOXnSVMKYFVyNwIFU8_ww-cBG6JIh7BhtDpU6TGdG9rcOlWy33HHa8B-8ua7gHkV0CDKf6UxUodTm_sADy-DpPgHMHPphVADnXk84K8xwcEjTURNUikaDlAnPnZxhB2Cl3d1rHhB7SMnBSYN4Tk2nwYxPsrf3FLZvpEkpUgBmPIvd4LOfKFOFqpkLtY7WAjOpsct3pjqzmGJcxA1RK1mFf9d8WRmw8RrMp0qF4WjAqlGgo3aCUhRqGiLe4kMAa-zS3BJXw866Uq_msv4PSG-zQAJuz1QmN_wwvEOSy8KGD4vyCmZWQeMnAAsaenAZ1qk-bQG9dz7Ju_zZ99vrRTYZTbTL0MCx7a35GosOgrmuIgyEaEJtZMzdxDChSR8UX_OkCiQl5FP_QueDq12Egc6ftZgdLQx6mujXRa1Tn4YtdxiaXlS7Y04F4dzO8zHFOKxgbqCVxwtX7l8cgiDmTP1rpekc7HMDiyO8mRhbqLNZBxNapAEsfNyLTmCHBnwKx0JQu7tD3rHvD5w0x2lzZfmBpZZyoxJaOgBsyXwuInhApQD-_OLGJCBr-KT6K-KLP__JH_MOxEOpHGIi8kM4Enii3sqWu2GHLaCw2IvWdUY%3D)** I wanted to follow up because this race is heating up quickly, and we can't let Bernie's political machine steamroll Minnesota.

Welcome to Collective PAC
Don't recognize this sender? [Unsubscribe with one click](https://substack..TykvdP8PJNxKzfBJ7c9rAEtWY_FKPbIgCjrlEIzgZbk?)
Collective PAC recently imported your email address from another platform to Substack. You'll now receive their posts via email or the [Substack app](https://link.sbstck.com/redirect/f749f1a9-7c79-4d81-b248-aa4b024fb7aa?j=eyJ1IjoiMWh2cjYwIn0.VBabVq-VHATXCJqsddI7uMpdiTm0WkVanRDZfvCWMkg). To set up your profile and discover more on Substack, [click here](https://link.sbstck.com/redirect/1465dbe9-d60b-4c8a-8e84-a68c42bd80a0?j=eyJ1IjoiMWh2cjYwIn0.VBabVq-VHATXCJqsddI7uMpdiTm0WkVanRDZfvCWMkg).
Hi there - it's Quentin James, Founder and President of The Collective PAC.
I'm reaching out to you via our new Substack page, where we're taking our mission of building Black political power further. We hope to create a space here where we can go deeper on the issues and talk about what's really at stake - in our country, in this upcoming election year, and beyond.
Today, I want to talk about the Voting Rights Act, the erosion of this landmark legislation, and what we must do to protect our sacred right to vote and our democracy itself.
Sixty years ago, the Voting Rights Act of 1965 became one of the most transformative pieces of legislation in American history. It was born from sacrifice, protest, and the unwavering insistence that a democracy cannot function when citizens are denied a voice. For decades, the VRA stood as a safeguard - an assurance that the right to vote would not be contingent on race, geography, or the whims of Republicans determined to silence our voices.
But today, that safeguard is eroding through a steady series of Supreme Court decisions that have hollowed out the core protections of the VRA. With each new decision dismantling a piece of the VRA, states have begun implementing new restrictions: strict ID laws, polling place closures, reduced early voting, aggressive voter roll purges, and more.
These changes are designed to burden voters - and they intentionally and disproportionately affect communities of color. Piece by piece, the VRA is being weakened. Piece by piece, the guardrails designed to ensure equal access to the ballot box are being removed.
Now, the Supreme Court is poised to gut the last remaining tenets of the Voting Rights Act - clearing the way for racist gerrymandering and allowing our voices to be drowned out. We cannot sit idly by and watch it happen.
A recent poll shows that three in four Americans believe democracy is at risk. They're right. But here's what we must remember: the Voting Rights Act itself was not inevitable. It was demanded into existence. It was built by people who refused to accept a democracy that worked for some but not for all. We must continue that fight.
This moment calls for a collective response - from organizations, from voters, from legal advocates, and from elected leaders who recognize that the health of our democracy is inseparable from the strength of our voting rights protections.
That's why, for the past 10 years, The Collective PAC has been working to create a more representative democracy and fix the challenge of under-representation of the Black community in elected seats of power throughout our nation.
Rebuilding and defending democracy isn't a simple task, and it's not about one election - it's a long-term project that requires educating voters, countering disinformation, supporting candidates who champion voting rights, holding institutions accountable, and sustaining grassroots infrastructure that reaches voters year-round.
**[That's where you come in: We're doubling down on our grassroots organizing as we prepare for 2026 - and we need your help. Will you pitch in to our grassroots movement today? Every dollar you give helps us register Black voters, fight voter suppression, and build the power we need to protect our communities and our democracy.](https://link.sbstck.com/redirect/d9bb930b-3cf8-44e4-8eac-7a44193100e7?j=eyJ1IjoiMWh2cjYwIn0.VBabVq-VHATXCJqsddI7uMpdiTm0WkVanRDZfvCWMkg)**
[Donate](https://link.sbstck.com/redirect/d9bb930b-3cf8-44e4-8eac-7a44193100e7?j=eyJ1IjoiMWh2cjYwIn0.VBabVq-VHATXCJqsddI7uMpdiTm0WkVanRDZfvCWMkg)
Together, we can meet this moment with the urgency it deserves.
Thank you,
Quentin James
Founder & President, The Collective PAC

name: Fred Wellman
subject: I’ve lost everything
disclaimer_text: PAID FOR BY SHAWN FOR GEORGIA
body:
| | [ ](http://clicksp.shawnharrisforcongress.com/f/a/z6P9m7D5pD4iBVTxtNw97w~~/AAPPABA~/qDcAhxLDVAG5eQ1aFJUBteVCNlqt2ArOA4Ao-_vaBcl0cq7PFC7RTkjArkjHN3gT-QHBXtGlP6NVi52GLSF8Jegc7ZjDAmLnurMutKnKaQ3uLNKT7hZMchusWjwgkwHH2NfA_51qpxX25IFwTndnR4bhA1SEuc0oCebb1LtvVoLrcm3Yqt5R3QC3W3B7ovcZOMLgvhD-XfgJ-G_TNiFCBGF4j_vUbEEdZXxDLT9uZjtQhabfb-FajBj9cm6SyFhzNr2ZaIMIBb3oF_2WWxGJab2EdpdzT9ss1pAgoVlg0YQl13Iyobtu5cbFgsDvaW0V)
---
I lost everything in my life too many times to count. I lost my first wife and unborn child in a car crash. I've been through bankruptcy and a divorce. I've been to war four times and came back with the scars both mentally and physically that come with that. Just like so many Americans, I've been knocked down and gotten back up. **And now I 'm running for Congress in one of the most competitive districts in the country--against a Congresswoman who's done nothing other than sell us out. ** My name is Fred Wellman. I'm a 22-year Army veteran, entrepreneur, and dad running for Congress in Missouri's Second Congressional District. Ann Wagner hasn't held a town hall in years, she's tripled her net worth while in Congress, and she's been nothing other than a rubber stamp on Trump's agenda. **But I need your help to take this seat back for the people. Can you chip in $15, $5, or even just $3 to support my campaign?**

clean_body: | | I lost everything in my life too many times to count. I lost my first wife and unborn child in a car crash. I've been through bankruptcy and a divorce. I've been to war four times and came back with the scars both mentally and physically that come with that. Just like so many Americans, I've been knocked down and gotten back up. And now I 'm running for Congress in one of the most competitive districts in the country--against a Congresswoman who's done nothing other than sell us out. My name is Fred Wellman. I'm a 22-year Army veteran, entrepreneur, and dad running for Congress in Missouri's Second Congressional District. Ann Wagner hasn't held a town hall in years, she's tripled her net worth while in Congress, and she's been nothing other than a rubber stamp on Trump's agenda. But I need your help to take this seat back for the people. Can you chip in $15, $5, or even just $3 to support my campaign? ~~ ~~ ~~ Thank you, Fred Wellman Democratic candidate, MO-02 | | ~~ "Facebook" ~ "X" \[BLUESKY\] "Bluesky" PAID FOR BY SHAWN FOR GEORGIA
